If you have or suspect you have a cavity (e.g., you are experiencing toothache, tooth sensitivity, pain when eating or drinking, or stains), you should see a dental professional right away. The dental profession has a number of efficient ways to stop tooth decay and support the health of your teeth. Fillings are the most common form of treatment and involves removing the decayed part of the tooth and "filling" the region with composite resin, porcelain or other materials.Evidence in favor of natural treatments is extremely limited and dated. In fact, the one study that does suggest that a diet rich in fruits, vegetables, meat, milk, and vitamin D dates back to 1932!It's best to get the care you need as soon as possible. The earlier you get the cavity treated by a dental professional, the better the chances of preventing the progression of the cavity. Moreover, if a cavity is treated before you even experience any pain, you likely won't need more advanced - and costlier - treatments, like a root canal.;
, Instead, get plenty of sunshine (though you can't wear sunscreen to metabolize vitamin D so limit your time to a maximum of 15-30 minutes a session). During the winter months, when sun exposure may be less available to you, you can also take vitamin D supplements., Vitamin K2 is a naturally occurring compound similar to vitamin K that is integral in the development of facial bones, including the teeth. As it is generally lacking in modern diets, making a concerted effort to increase your intake may help heal your cavities naturally. Vitamin K2 is typically found in fermented foods and animal products such as:Animal (especially crab and lobster) innards
Skate liver oil
Bone marrow

, Some studies suggest that cavities occur in part due to a lack of fatty vitamins (vitamins A, D, and K) in modern diets.The fact that this fish oil is fermented, rather than distilled, means that it is still chock-full of vitamins D and A, both integral to the remineralization of your teeth.


If you can't get or don't want to try fermented cod liver oil, you can add more vitamin A to your diet by eating large amounts of chicken liver or goat cheese, or drinking whole milk. Keep in mind that it will take 2 ounces of liver, 17 ounces of goat cheese, and two gallons of milk to equal just one teaspoon of fermented cod liver oil.Likewise, you can add more vitamin D to your diet by eating large amounts of salmon, eggs, and again, drinking whole milk. To equal the amount of vitamin D in one teaspoon of fermented cod liver oil, you'll need to eat 18 ounces of salmon, 5 dozen eggs, and 21 gallons (79.5 L) of whole milk., Calcium helps strengthen teeth, so increase your calcium servings. The easiest way to do that is to consume more dairy products, like milk, cheese, and yogurt. Calcium can help remineralize your teeth.If you can, try eating cheese. Cheese helps stimulate saliva which in turn restores minerals to the teeth and washes away remaining food particles.

, You can buy fluoride-free toothpastes that can also help remineralize the teeth and make them stronger. Note that these toothpastes may be more expensive than your usual brand.You can also make your own mineralizing toothpaste if you want to save some money. combine four tablespoons of coconut oil, two tablespoons of baking soda, one tablespoon of xylitol (or 1/8 teaspoon of stevia), 20 drops of peppermint oil and 20 drops of trace minerals or calcium/magnesium powder., When you have a cavity, bacteria and acid will stain your tooth. Variation in color indicates the extent of the damage; a darker color means a larger cavity. When you are working to heal a cavity, check back and see if you notice a change in tooth color.


In addition, consider the sensation of pain. If the pain seems to be changing from a long-lasting, throbbing pain to a more incidental pain or sensitivity to hot and cold foods, the cavity may be improving. However, if the pain intensifies, you should consult your dentists for treatment.
Note any food impaction. When a tooth fractures, food can get stuck inside the cavity. This creates a wedging effect and causes discomfort and sensitivity. In addition, it can greatly impair the healing process.
Watch for fractures. Depending on the size of your original cavity, your cavity-filled tooth may be significantly weaker than a normal, healthy one. If you are opting not to get dental treatment, be especially aware of this.

, You should brush your teeth at least twice a day. Ideally, you should brush your teeth 30 minutes after eating and after drinking anything other than water. Keep the toothbrush at a 45 degree angle to your gums and gently move the toothbrush back and forth in short strokes. Make sure you brush the inner, outer and chewing surfaces of the teeth.


Don’t forget to brush your tongue as the tongue can also carry bacteria and food particles.Use a soft toothbrush. Your teeth can be damaged by brushing too hard or using toothbrushes with hard bristles. You should replace your toothbrush every three to four months.
Leave the toothpaste in your mouth without rinsing. Spit out the extra foam, but don’t rinse your mouth out with water. You want to give the minerals in the toothpaste some time to be absorbed onto your teeth.
If your teeth are sensitive, use a toothpaste for sensitive teeth--these can help reduce gum inflammation as well., Using about 18 inches of floss, wind most of the floss around the middle finger of one hand and the rest around the middle finger of the other hand. Hold on to the floss firmly between your thumb and your forefinger. Gently guide the floss between ALL your teeth using a gently back and forth motion. Be sure to curve the floss around the bottom of each tooth. Once the floss is between the teeth, use an up-and-down motion (gently!) to rub each side of each tooth. When you are done with one tooth, unwind more floss and move on to the next tooth.If you're uncertain about the proper flossing technique, you can watch a video produced by the American Dental Association.

, Fluoride in toothpaste and dental rinses replaces the calcium component in hydroxyapatite with fluorapatite, a substance that is resistant to demineralization by acids and therefore helps prevent cavities.Fluoride in toothpaste helps strengthens the enamel. Fluoride can also help the process of preventing cavities because it is anti-microbial, killing off the oral bacteria that is the underlying cause or cavities.Although some have expressed concern about the use of fluoride, the 2007 National Research Council report indicated that fluoride is an essential mineral and is healthy and necessary for tooth and bone structure.
You can also use a special toothpaste meant to rebuild enamel, such as Squigle Enamel Saver toothpaste (with fluoride). Note that you can use fluoride-free toothpastes, but it does appear that they may put you at higher risk for cavities., Make sure you see a dentist and get your teeth professionally cleaned by a dental hygienist at least every six months. However, there is no one-size-fits-all in terms of how frequently you should go to the dentist. For example, if you have deep grooves between your teeth, your dentist may even want you to come in for a cleaning and inspection every four months.Follow your dentist's guidelines on how to properly care for your teeth and their particular structure and makeup.

, Snacking throughout the day or sipping drinks here and there mean your teeth are constantly at risk. Each time you eat or drink something (other than water), your mouth bacteria creates acids that work to break down your tooth enamel.If you must snack, make a healthy choice like cheese, yogurt, or a piece of fruit. Avoid snack foods that are unfriendly to your teeth sugar such as chips or sweets.

, Cavity-causing bacteria need food (namely, carbohydrates and sugar) to survive. They then turn that food into acid, which weakens the teeth. Limit your intake of carbs and sugar so that the bacteria do not have anything to feed on. This means trying to avoid all processed and prepackaged food, such as cookies, cakes, chips, crackers, etc.You should also avoid soda and any other sweetened drinks because these foods tend to contain a lot of added sugar. In addition, sodas are very acidic and can damage the enamel on your teeth.If still want to enjoy something sweet, try using honey, which is anti-bacterial. You could also use stevia, which is an herb that is 200 times sweeter than sugar.
To satisfy your urge to eat grains, try fermented grains, like real sourdough bread, and only in moderation.When you do indulge in carbohydrates or sugar, be sure to brush your teeth afterwards to clean them off debris which can stick to your teeth and hasten the development of decay.

, Most fruit contain another type of sugar that is not as popular with bacteria so enjoy that apple, pear, peach or any other fruit. In addition, fresh fruits, along with vegetables, can increase saliva flow and help wash away food debris on you teeth.Try to moderate the amounts of citrus fruits you eat as these are quite acidic and can break down tooth enamel over time. Eat them as part of a meal (and not by themselves) and always rinse your mouth out with water afterwards to wash away the food particles., Chewing stimulates the production of saliva, which is naturally anti-bacterial and helps to wash away the food particles that stick to the teeth. Saliva contains calcium and phosphate and can help neutralize the acids in food and destroy some bacteria.


Sour foods tend to increase saliva production, but sour foods are also acidic, so chew, chew and chew some more to increase the amount of saliva you make., There are some who recommend that you minimize foods that contain phytic acid (such as beans and legumes) on the basis of the idea that phytic acid prevents the absorption of minerals. There is essentially no scientific basis for this and it appears to be an anecdotal myth. Phytic acid does bind minerals, but those minerals are released by cooking, by soaking beans and legumes in water prior to cooking and in the acidic environment of the stomach. In other words, you do not need to try to limit these foods in order to prevent cavities., If you are taking a multivitamin, make sure it includes minerals, particularly calcium and magnesium. Remember, calcium and magnesium (and especially calcium, the main mineral in your teeth) are important for strong teeth.In general a mineral supplement should contain:


Enough calcium that you get at least 1000 mg every day. (Men older than 71 and women older than 51 should get 1200 mg every day).
Enough magnesium so that you get about 300-400 mg every day. Children have different needs (for children from birth to 3 years, 40-80 mg/day; for kids between 3-6 yrs, 120 mg/day; for kids up to 10 years, 170 mg/day) For children, use children’s vitamins.

, These include fatty fish (like salmon, mackerel and tuna), soymilk, coconut milk, cow’s milk, eggs, and yogurt. Another way to get vitamin D is through sun exposure or a supplement that you can buy at any health food store or pharmacy.Adults and children should get about 600 IU (International Units) of vitamin D every day. Adults over 70 years should get 800 IU every day.

, Water—particularly fluoridated water— is considered by some as best drink for tooth health. The general recommendation is about eight glasses of tap water a day. Most public water systems have added fluoride to help prevent tooth decay. Drinking water helps you stay hydrated so you can keep producing enough saliva. In addition, water helps wash down loose food debris.There is a lot of controversy around fluoridated water. It is unclear how great an effect fluoridated water has on dental health, and some have concerns about potential side effects of drinking fluoride and prolonged exposure., Anti-bacterial herbs can be used to help control the bacteria in your mouth and prevent their growth. Some of the most effective antibacterial herbs include clove, thyme, goldenseal, oregon grape root, and oregano. You can make concentrated teas using any of these herbs or dilute them to use as a mouthwash.To make tea: Boil water and pour into a covered bowl. Use two teaspoons of dried herb for every two cups of water. Gently stir the herbs and cover the bowl. Let the water cool completely and then pour the concentrated tea into a jar with a lid through a sieve (to catch the dried herb) and refrigerate. You can use these for up to two weeks after refrigeration.

To make mouthwash: When you want an antibacterial mouthwash, get a glass and add equal parts of concentrated tea and water. Use this as a rinse. Keep it in your mouth for one to two minutes and do not rinse with water for about five minutes.

How to Make up After an Argument

Arguments are a natural part of any relationship. Regardless of the severity or the frequency, what’s important is how we handle them. By implementing the right strategies, it’s possible to make up after an argument and strengthen your bond with the other person. Here are the steps to make up after an argument:

1. Apologize immediately if possible.

If you realize you’ve made a mistake, it’s best to apologize right away. This shows the other person that you regret your actions and that you value the relationship. Be sure to apologize sincerely and take responsibility for your actions.

2. Wait until you are calm before making up.

If you’re feeling angry or emotional, it might be best to wait until you’ve calmed down before trying to make up. This will help you approach the situation with a clear head and avoid saying things you don’t mean.

3. Manage your impulses.

When emotions run high, it can be difficult to control our impulses. However, it’s important to keep in mind that lashing out or making accusations will only make the situation worse. Instead, take a deep breath and focus on calming down before trying to make amends.

4. Separate your feelings from the issue.

It’s important to distinguish between your feelings and the issue at hand. By separating the two, you can focus on resolving the problem rather than getting caught up in your emotions.

5. Avoid discounting the other person’s feelings.

Just because you don’t agree with someone’s perspective doesn’t mean their feelings aren’t valid. Make an effort to understand where the other person is coming from, even if you don’t necessarily agree with them.

6. Avoid mind-reading.

Assuming you know what someone else is thinking or feeling can lead to misunderstandings. Instead, ask the other person to express their thoughts and feelings in their own words.

7. Write down your feelings.

Putting your thoughts and feelings down on paper can help you get a clearer perspective on the situation. Writing can also help you organize your thoughts and express them more effectively.

8. Choose your moment wisely.

Timing is everything when it comes to making up after an argument. Choose a time when both of you are calm and can focus on the issue at hand.

9. Arrange to meet with them in person, if possible.

While it’s possible to make up over the phone or online, meeting with someone in person can help you communicate more effectively and resolve the issue more quickly.

10. Frame your invitation as an offer, not a demand.

Instead of demanding that the other person meet with you, frame your invitation as an offer. This will make them more likely to accept and help create a positive atmosphere.

11. Give the other person space to talk.

Letting the other person express their thoughts and feelings can help you understand where they’re coming from and find a resolution to the problem.

12. Listen to what the other person has to say.

Active listening is key to any effective communication. Make sure you’re focused on what the other person is saying and avoid interrupting or tuning out.

13. Verbalize your understanding of the other person’s feelings.

Repeating what the other person has said in your own words can help them feel heard and understood.

14. Remember the “Three R’s”.

When making up after an argument, it’s important to remember the “Three R’s”: recognize, regret, and resolve. Recognize and acknowledge the other person’s feelings, express regret for your actions, and work together to resolve the issue.

15. Express empathy for the other person.

Putting yourself in the other person’s shoes can help you understand their perspective and create a more positive atmosphere for making up.

16. Use “I” instead of “You.” Keep the focus on what you did and how you felt, rather than making accusations about the other person.

By using “I” statements, you can take responsibility for your actions and avoid making the other person feel defensive.

17. Don’t just say “I’m sorry”.

While saying “I’m sorry” is a good start, it’s important to follow up with actions that show you’re committed to making amends.

18. Avoid the “but”.

Using the word “but” can feel like an excuse for your actions and negate the apology.

19. Don’t stand your ground.

Standing your ground and refusing to acknowledge the other person’s feelings will only prolong the argument and create more tension.

20. Talk about the future.

After resolving the issue, it’s important to talk about the future and how you can work together to prevent similar conflicts from happening again.

21. Avoid making promises you can’t keep.

If you promise to change something, make sure it’s something you can realistically follow through on.

22. Suggest a pleasant activity to do together.

Doing something enjoyable together can help reinforce the relationship and create positive memories.

23. Talk about what caused the argument.

Understanding what caused the argument can help prevent similar conflicts from happening again in the future.

24. Prioritize communication.

Effective communication is key to any healthy relationship. Make an effort to communicate openly and honestly with the other person.

25. Check in with the other person.

After making up, it’s important to check in with the other person and make sure they’re feeling okay.

26. Understand that a certain degree of conflict is normal.

No relationship is without conflict. Understanding that disagreements are a natural part of any relationship can help you approach them with a more positive attitude.

Los Angeles is one of the top tourist destinations in the world, with a plethora of entertainment and activities for all ages and interests. Whether you are looking for a beach vacation, theme park adventure, or an art and culture-filled trip, Los Angeles has something to offer.

1. Decide the focus of your vacation
Before planning your Los Angeles vacation, decide on the focus of your trip. Do you want to spend time at the beach, visit theme parks, explore the city's art and culture scene, or go on a shopping spree? Once you have decided the focus of your trip, you can tailor your itinerary accordingly.

2. Theme park vacation: There are 7 major theme parks in Southern California!
A theme park vacation is a quintessential Los Angeles experience. Southern California is home to seven major theme parks, including Disneyland, Universal Studios Hollywood, Knott's Berry Farm, Six Flags Magic Mountain, Legoland California, SeaWorld San Diego, and San Diego Zoo Safari Park. Whether you are a fan of roller coasters, movie studios, or marine animals, there is a theme park that caters to your interests.

3. Beach vacations: You'll enjoy a continuous stretch of beach in Southern California.
Los Angeles boasts a stunning stretch of coastline, with numerous beaches to choose from. The world-famous Santa Monica Beach, Venice Beach, Malibu, and Huntington Beach are just a few of the many idyllic spots to soak up the sun, surf, and sand. Take a leisurely stroll along the boardwalk, grab a bite to eat at a beachside cafe, or dip your toes in the Pacific Ocean.

4. Hollywood vacations: See the Hollywood sign...
A trip to Los Angeles wouldn't be complete without a visit to Hollywood. Marvel at the iconic Hollywood sign, hunt for stars on the Hollywood Walk of Fame, and catch a concert at the Hollywood Bowl. Take a tour of the Kodak Theater, home of the Academy Awards, and shop and dine at the outrageous, "Babylon" themed Hollywood & Highland Center. Don't miss the restored Wurlitzer and take in a Disney movie at the El Capitan Theater, put your feet into famous footprints in the Grauman's Chinese Theater courtyard, see the Hollywood tourist overview movie at the historic Egyptian Theater, or check out the newly restored Griffith Park Observatory.

5. Mountain vacation: Take a drive...
Escape the hustle and bustle of the city with a mountain vacation. Take a drive on the isolated Angeles Crest Highway, enjoy the stunning scenery of the San Gabriel Mountains, or hike to the Hollywood Sign. Enjoy a picnic at one of the mountain's many parks, go for a scenic drive, or hit the slopes during the winter season.

6. Shopping vacation: Rodeo Drive in Beverly Hills...
Los Angeles is a shopper's paradise, with plenty of high-end shopping destinations. Beverly Hills' Rodeo Drive is a must-visit for fashion-lovers, while The Grove, Westfield Century City, and Westfield Topanga offer an array of shopping, dining, and entertainment options. Don't miss the trendy boutiques on Melrose Avenue and the vintage shops on Sunset Boulevard.

7. Island vacation: Catalina Island...
Escape the mainland and head to Catalina Island, located, "26 miles (42 km) across the sea," as the old song says. This idyllic island paradise is just an hour's ferry ride from Long Beach or San Pedro. Enjoy the crystal-clear waters, zipline across the island's rugged landscapes, or explore the charming town of Avalon.

8. History vacation: Visit Olvera Street in downtown Los Angeles...
Discover the rich history of Los Angeles by visiting Olvera Street in downtown LA, where the city started. This vibrant Mexican marketplace is home to a variety of shops, eateries, and cultural events. The nearby Union Station is another historic landmark worth visiting, with beautiful Art Deco architecture and a bustling transit hub.

9. Art vacation: Don't miss the still-new Getty Museum...
Los Angeles is a hub for art and culture, with numerous museums and galleries to explore. The Getty Museum is a must-visit for art-lovers, with its impressive collection of European paintings, sculptures, and decorative arts. The original Getty Museum, located in Malibu, is a stunning architectural masterpiece with breathtaking views of the Pacific Ocean. Other noteworthy museums include the Norton Simon Museum in Pasadena and the Los Angeles County Museum of Art.

10. Animal vacations: Don't miss the San Diego Zoo...
Animal-lovers will enjoy the many wildlife attractions that Los Angeles has to offer. The San Diego Zoo is one of the world's foremost zoological parks, while the San Diego Wildlife Park in Escondido offers a unique safari experience. SeaWorld San Diego is another popular destination, with lovable Shamu the Killer Whale. Don't forget to take your furry friend to "Dog Beach" in Huntington Beach, before dining together at the Park Bench Cafe in HB's Central Park (where you'll find both human AND dog menus!).

How to Calculate the Day of the Week

Calculating the day of the week can be a useful skill in various situations, such as planning events or scheduling tasks. In order to calculate the day of the week, you need to have an understanding of how the Gregorian calendar works. Here are some useful properties of the Gregorian calendar and how they can be used to calculate the day of the week:

1. Years
- Years divisible by 4 are leap years, which means that they have an extra day (February 29) added to them.
- However, years divisible by 100 are not leap years, with the exception of years divisible by 400, which are leap years.

2. Months
- January, March, May, July, August, October, and December have 31 days.
- April, June, September, and November have 30 days.
- February has 28 days, except for leap years when it has 29 days.

3. Days
- In any given year (even a leap year), "Doomsdays" are all on the same day of the week. Doomsdays are the days that fall on January 3, February 28/29, March 7, April 4, May 9, June 6, July 11, August 8, September 5, October 10, November 7, and December 12.

Now that you know how the Gregorian calendar works, you can use your knowledge to calculate the day of the week.

Calculate the Day of the Week in Your Head From the Year, Month, and Day
To calculate the day of the week for any given date, you need to know the Year-day, Century-day, Quad-day, and Doomsday for that year. Here are the steps to follow:

1. Find the Year-day
- The Year-day of any given year is the day of the week on which all of its Doomsdays lie.
- To find the Year-day, first determine the Doomsday for the year (using the Doomsday algorithm below).
- Then, count the number of days between the first Doomsday of the year and the nearest Doomsday to the date you are trying to calculate.
- Divide the number of days by 7, and take the remainder. This will give you the Year-day.

2. Find the Century-day
- The Century-day is a number that is associated with the century that the year is in.
- To find the Century-day, first determine which century the year is in (i.e. the first two digits of the year).
- Then, use the table below to find the Century-day for that century:

Century | Century-day
--------|------------
1700s   | 5
1800s   | 3
1900s   | 1
2000s   | 0
2100s   | 6
2200s   | 4
2300s   | 2

3. Find the Quad-day
- The Quad-day is a number that is associated with the year and is used to calculate the Doomsday.
- To find the Quad-day, find the greatest number that is both less than or equal to the given year and divisible by 4.

4. Find the Doomsday
- The Doomsday is the day of the week that falls on all of the Doomsdays for the year.
- To find the Doomsday, use the following algorithm:
   - Determine the Quad-day for the year.
   - Divide the last two digits of the year by 12, and take the remainder.
   - Add the remainder to the Quad-day.
   - If the year is a leap year and the Quad-day is greater than February 28, subtract 1 from the Quad-day.
   - Divide the Quad-day by 7, and take the remainder. This will give you the Doomsday.

5. Calculate the Day of the Week
- Once you have the Year-day, Century-day, Quad-day, and Doomsday, you can use the following formula to calculate the day of the week:
   - Day of the week = (Year-day + Century-day + Quad-day + Day of the Month + Adjustment) % 7
   - Adjustment = 0 for January and February of a non-leap year, 1 for January of a leap year, and 2 for February of a leap year.

By following these steps, you can calculate the day of the week for any given date.

Do you love the world of The Hunger Games and want to create a teenage Sim in The Sims 3 that seems like a District 4 tribute? Look no further, as this article will guide you through all the steps you need to take to make the perfect teenage tribute Sim!

Step 1: Generate the Name

The first thing you'll need to do is generate a name for your teenage Sim. You can use a generator from websites like FantasyNameGenerators.com or find your Hunger Games name online.

Step 2: Adjust the Muscle Level

Set your teenage Sim's muscle level to as high as it will go. District 4 tributes are known for their strength and athleticism, so make sure your Sim looks like he or she could hold his or her own in the arena.

Step 3: Choose a Skin Color

Your Sim's skin color should be any color except for pale. Try a more olive or tan skin tone to represent being outdoors in the sun.

Step 4: Select the Right Hair Color

For the hair color, choose either blond (to represent sun-bleached hair) or black (to represent islanders and therefore "beachiness"). Avoid anything in between.

Step 5: Pick the Right Eye Color

Give your tribute Sim green eyes. This will give them a unique and striking appearance.

Step 6: Use Subtle Makeup

If your tribute Sim is female, use a subdued eyeliner and a subtly tinted lipstick. This will help them look well put-together while still being practical for the arena.

Step 7: Dress Appropriately

Dress your tribute Sim in denim shorts, a t-shirt or tank top in blue, green, gray, white, or navy, and flip flops. This will give them a casual but functional appearance.

Step 8: Choose the Right Traits

Give your District 4 teenage Sim the traits Angler, Snob, Athletic, and either Mean Spirited or Evil. These traits represent the skills and attitude needed to succeed as a District 4 tribute.

Step 9: Pick the Perfect Favorite Food

For their favorite food, choose sushi, grilled salmon, or fish and chips. These foods represent the fishing district of District 4.

Step 10: Set the Favorite Color

Set your tribute Sim's favorite color to an aquatic color. This color will reflect their background in District 4.

Step 11: Create a District 4 Environment

Move the tribute and his or her parent into a house with a pool. Delete all easels, guitars, and block tables and set the color of all lights in the tribute's room to blue, green or cyan. Put every possible fishbowl, treadmill, sports ball and aquatic themed piece of art around the house to really give it that District 4 feel.

Congratulations! You've successfully created a teenage Sim who seems like a District 4 tribute. Now you can put them in the arena and see if they have what it takes to survive!

Be sure to keep this up to date and do weekly full scans and updates.;
, This will allow your computer to automatically download any updates to both the operating system (i.e. Windows) and Internet Explorer. These updates fix security holes in both pieces of software.

,, To further reduce the possibility of getting a virus or similar please read the following advice:,, Here are 2 scenarios:

, You get an Email from someone you DON'T know. You open it. It tells you (or, persuades you) to click on a link in the Email. You do so. That is when you get infected. Frequently, the Email appears to be from a bank or a company you know. Do not fall for this. Businesses do not normally send unsolicited Email.

, You get (what appears to be) an Email from someone you DO know. Unknown to you, a virus generated that Email (and not your friend). It could be that your friend's computer is infected, but, not always. Obviously, the actual Email writer doesn't know you and cannot say anything personal to you, so, typically, it says something like "Click on this link for some important information... “. You are now infected.

,, The same caution should be used with opening links and attachments as Emails.

, In addition, do not download software or “Add-ons” from web sites that you are unfamiliar with. This includes sites such as “Facebook” and “MySpace”.

,,, Of course, you can run a virus scan on it first, but Anti-Virus programs are not 100% effective.

,, This includes torrents or other forms of P2P activities (Limewire for example). Staying away from these sites and programs is in your computer's health's best interest, as well as a good way to avoid being sued for copyright violation.

A fiberglass tub or shower can be a durable, long-lasting feature in your bathroom. However, over time, accidents can happen, and the surface can become damaged, leading to unsightly discoloration or even cracks. The good news is that repairing a fiberglass tub or shower is a relatively simple task that can be done on your own with the right tools and products. In this article, we'll guide you through the steps to repair a fiberglass tub or shower, using a suitable kit.

Part 1: Preparing for the Repair

1. Purchase a suitable kit for your shower (or bathtub).
Before starting any repair work, it's important to have a suitable kit that contains everything you need for the job. You can purchase fiberglass repair kits at most home improvement stores or online. Be sure to read the label of the kit carefully, to ensure that it's designed for repairing fiberglass tubs or showers.

2. Clean the area to be repaired.
Before applying any repair material, it's important to clean the damaged area carefully. Use a mixture of warm water and soap to clean the surface thoroughly, removing any grease, dirt, or mold. Rinse the area with clean water and let it dry completely before proceeding.

3. Determine if the damaged area will require fiberglass cloth reinforcing.
If the damage to your fiberglass tub or shower is more substantial, you may need to reinforce the area with fiberglass cloth. This will help create a stronger bond between the repair material and the tub or shower, ensuring that it won't crack or become dislodged over time.

4. Read the mixing and tinting instructions of the product you have purchased.
Before starting the repair, read the instructions included with the kit carefully. Take note of the mixing ratios and tinting instructions, so that you can achieve the desired color for your repair.

5. Place a protective material like cardboard or heavy construction paper on the surface you will mix the material on.
To protect your work surface from damage, place a protective material like cardboard or heavy construction paper on the surface where you'll be mixing the repair material.

Part 2: Making the Repair

1. Measure the amount of polyester resin into the mixing container, you think you will need for your repair.
Follow the instructions in the kit to measure the appropriate amount of polyester resin into a mixing container.

2. Add the colorant from the correct tube that came with the kit.
Use the correct colorant tube provided in the kit to add the desired color to the resin.

3. Mix thickening material into the resin/colorant mixture until it is a desired consistency for your patch.
Follow the instructions in the kit to add thickening material to the resin/colorant mixture, until it reaches a desired consistency for your patch.

4. Add the hardener according to the kit's instructions.
Use the hardener provided in the kit to activate the resin and ensure that it cures correctly.

5. Mix the repair material thoroughly.
Use a mixing tool to blend the repair material together until it is fully mixed and ready to be applied.

6. Using a flat tool like a putty knife or wooden tongue depressor, scoop out some of the mixture and apply it to the damaged area.
Using a flat tool like a putty knife or wooden tongue depressor, apply the repair material to the damaged area, spreading it evenly and leaving no gaps.

7. Sand the patch carefully, trying not to scuff the adjacent areas.
Once the patch has dried, use sandpaper to smooth out any rough edges or bumps, taking care not to scuff the adjacent areas.

8. Mix another batch of resin and colorant to overlay the first patch, without the thickening agent.
Mix another batch of resin and colorant according to the kit's instructions, but without thickening material. Apply this mixture over the first patch to blend it into the surrounding area.

9. Buff the finished repair with the buffing compound provided in the kit to restore the gloss finish so it matches the original.
Using the buffing compound provided in the kit, buff the finished repair until it matches the original gloss finish of the tub or shower.

Part 3: Finishing Up

1. Clean up the area, and admire your handiwork.
Once the repair has fully cured, clean up any remaining materials or debris from the area. Admire your handiwork and enjoy your newly-repaired fiberglass tub or shower!

Looking like a tart with a heart may seem like a daunting task, but with the right style and attitude, anyone can pull it off. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to achieve this look:

Hair:

Your hair should be long, straight, and glossy. If your hair is naturally curly or wavy, straighten it with a flat iron. Use a shine serum to add gloss to your hair.

Skin:

To keep your skin looking spot-free, use a gentle facial cleanser twice a day and moisturize daily. You can also use a light foundation to even out your skin tone.

Eyes:

The key to this look is dramatic eye makeup, even during the daytime. Use a black or dark brown eyeliner to create a winged look, and finish off with mascara to make your lashes look long and full.

Eyebrows:

Keep your eyebrows plucked at all times. Aim for a thin, short eyebrow shape that complements your facial features.

Lips:

To achieve pouty, glossy lips, use a lip plumper and top it off with a high-shine gloss.

Nails:

Keep your nails fairly long but not too long that they look like talons. File them into a squoval shape, which is square with rounded corners.

Piercings:

Always wear earrings, even if you don't have pierced ears. Clip-ons are a great alternative.

Underwear:

Wearing nice underwear is a must. Choose underwear that makes you feel confident and sexy.

Tops:

Opt for low-cut, revealing tops and corsets. Look for ones with lace detailing around the cleavage area to add a touch of femininity.

Skirts/Trousers:

Skirts are the best choice for this look. Wear skirts that are super short and show off your legs. You can wear any style you want, from mini skirts to pencil skirts.

Shoes:

Always wear high heels. If you can't balance in stilettos, opt for platforms instead.

Coats:

Wear short coats that finish at your waist. Make sure they are relatively tight-fitting to show off your figure.

Accessories:

When it comes to jewelry, silver is the way to go. Add some sparkle to your look with hoop earrings, a necklace, and a bracelet.

Overall, looking like a tart with a heart is all about being confident, bold, and unapologetically sexy. Embrace your inner vixen and embrace this look with style and confidence!

Vacuum frequently. Using a vacuum with a high-efficiency particulate air filter (HEPA) will reduce the allergens that are in the air.
Reduce the number of carpets you have in your house. Carpets, in contrast to hard floors, hold allergens and pet dander, making it more difficult to keep the house allergen-free.
Wash your bedding regularly. You spend about a third of your day in bed. If you have allergens on your sheets and pillow, you are spending a third of your time breathing in those allergens. Use a plastic cover on your mattress to prevent allergens from settling.

An online search including “district attorney” and the county or city name will almost always turn up an email and physical address. Alternately, you can call or visit the country or city court to request this information.


If you are looking for the specific attorney handling your case, it is best to email or call the county or city court clerk to find this information.;
, For best results, you will want to write a formal letter and address the district attorney appropriately.The envelope: The Honorable (Full name), District Attorney of (city or county)
Letter salutation: Dear Mr. / Madame District Attorney:

, Make your letter as brief as possible while still including all the required information. Even if you are upset – e.g. you are a victim writing because our case is not being prosecuted – try to keep you letter formal and respectful. This is the best way to ensure a positive response., If you are a defendant – in traffic court, for instance – and wish for more time to prepare your defense, you can sometimes write the district attorney for a continuance, though in some jurisdictions, you will need to appear in person to do so.State the reason you need a continuance – i.e. to prepare your defense – in your letter.

, All 50 states allow victim impact statements, which allow victims to indicate the toll the crime has take on them, and which may impact the judge’s sentence. These statements may include descriptions of:Injury caused by the crime
Emotional damage caused by the crime
Financial cost of the crime
Medical or psychological treatments required by the victim
The victim’s views on the crime and the appropriate punishment

, During the course of a trial, you may need to send written statements or other information to the district attorney, or ask questions.

, Anything you write to the district attorney may be admissible as evidence in your case. Accordingly, your lawyer should handle all communication with the prosecution.

, In some states, you will need to write to a special agency to obtain these records, but in most states, you can write to your local district attorney. You can find a form information request letter for each state here. You will typically have to pay a per-page fee for any document produced. Be sure to include as much information as possible, including:Name of defendant(s)
Indictment or docket number
Approximate date of the trial

, If a crime is happening at that moment, call 911. Otherwise, write or call your local police department to report most crimes, including theft, vandalism, assault, or nuisance complaints.

, Cases of public corruption should be brought directly to the attention of the district attorney. Give as much information as you can on the crime. The cases DAs will investigate vary from jurisdiction to jurisdiction, but often include:Public corruption
Criminal acts by members of the judiciary
Election violations
Fraud involving large amounts (typically over $300,000)
Environmental crimes

, DAs typically have 1 to 2 years to bring charges, and a trial may be delayed by workload or the need to gather evidence.That said, if you are a victim and feel that the DA is failing to pursue your case, you can write a letter encouraging him or her to do so. For best results, be sure to add a cc list, letting the DA know that you have also sent the letter to other important people, including his or her boss. People to consider putting in your cc: include:Local officials – the mayor, city council members, judges, etc.
Local leaders- A prominent clergy person, business leaders, etc.
Appropriate organizations and issue Groups – NAACP, National Organization for Victim Assistance, American Civil Liberties Union, National Organization for Women, etc.
State and national officials – attorney general, state and national congress people, governor
Local newspapers and journalists

, All states have laws that require the government to give citizens access to public records. If the government fails to turn over records, the next step is usually to contact the local district attorney to lodge a criminal complaint.Be sure to specify as precisely as possible the documents you requested and to include a copy of any rejection notice sent to you.

, Even if you were at fault, DAs will often reduce the level of the fine or change the nature of the charge so no points accrue to your license if you have a previously clean driving record. There is no guarantee your letter will succeed, but as it only costs you a few minutes and a stamp, it’s worth a try.

, The DA will likely contact the officer who pulled you over to ask about the incident. Officers take notes on each stop, and if you were rude, he will let the DA know. That will likely mean the failure of your request, since the success of your letter depends on the DA’s good will., You can call or email the clerk of the court for the county or city where you received your ticket to discover the District Attorney for your case.

, A clean driving record can help convince the DA to reduce the penalty against you. The state department that issues drivers licenses will also be able to provide you with your driving record. See here for information on how to obtain your record in your state.

, Keep your letter short and to the point. There are several things you will want to include:Mention that you are not contesting your speed or the speed zone in the area.
Explain why you were speeding – i.e. you did not realize the new speed limit after transitioning from a faster speed zone.
Apologize and indicate that you will watch your speed more closely in the future.
Mention your clean driving record and attach it.
Ask for a reduced fine and/or a reduction in charges to a non-point violation.

Mark where the corners/bends will be with a marker, allowing for a curve radius of about 6 inches (15.2 cm).
Heat up the marked section of pipe with a flame, such as a blowtorch or gas stove.
Do not breathe the smoke/fumes from the heated pipe.
Move the pipe constantly to heat the entire area.

Continue for the other bends.

Have you ever wondered how to calculate food cost for your restaurant, food truck, or catering business? Understanding this calculation is essential to running a successful and profitable food business. In this article, we'll guide you through the step-by-step process of calculating food costs.

Understand why you need this calculation.

Calculating your food cost is crucial to keeping your business profitable. The maximum tells you what percentage of your business's operating budget can be allotted to food cost for the operation to still turn a profit. By keeping track of your food cost, you will be able to make pricing adjustments, reduce waste, and make informed purchasing decisions.

Begin by calculating your operating budget.

To determine your food cost, you need to first calculate your operating budget. This should include all of your fixed and variable costs, such as rent, utilities, salaries, and advertising expenses.

Determine how much money you can afford to spend each month.

After calculating your operating budget, determine how much money you can afford to spend on food each month. This will help you set a budget for purchasing ingredients.

Calculate a budget percentage for each of these costs.

Once you've determined your overall food budget, calculate a budget percentage for each of the costs associated with running your food business. This should include expenses such as purchasing ingredients, labor costs, packaging, and delivery costs.

Figure out your maximum allowable food cost per month.

Once you have a percentage for each of these amounts, add those amounts up. This will give you your maximum allowable food cost per month.

Choose a date that will begin each weekly assessment period for you.

To keep track of your food cost, choose a date that will begin each weekly assessment period for you. This will make it easier to track your inventory and purchases.

Determine your “opening inventory.”

On the day that begins your “fiscal week” — Sunday, in our case — do a thorough inspection of all the food products in your kitchen. This will give you an accurate opening inventory count for the week.

Track your purchases.

Track all purchases made during the weekly assessment period. This will include all the food products you purchase for your business.

Take inventory again at the beginning of your next fiscal week.

At the beginning of the next fiscal week, take another inventory count. This will help you keep track of how much inventory you've used throughout the week.

Find out how much you made in food sales during the week.

At the end of the week, find out how much you made in food sales during the week. This will give you an accurate sales figure for the week.

Calculate your actual food cost for the week.

To calculate your actual food cost for the week, subtract your closing inventory from your opening inventory, add the total amount of purchases made during the week, and subtract that number from the total sales figure for the week.

Compare your maximum allowable and actual food costs.

Compare your maximum allowable food cost per month to your actual food cost for the week. This will help you determine if you're staying within your budget.

Calculate your total cost.

To calculate your total cost, add up all of your expenses, including ingredients, labor, packaging, and delivery costs.

Figure out your total sales.

Calculating your total sales is essential to understanding your profit margin. Sum up all of your sales figures over a given period of time to determine your total sales.

Find out your potential food cost.

Your potential food cost is the cost of ingredients that should have been used to create the dishes you sold that week. To find out your potential food cost, add up the cost of the ingredients in each dish you sold that week.

Analyze your potential food cost.

By analyzing your potential food cost, you can get insights into how efficient your kitchen is, and whether or not you're cutting costs in the right places. Adjust your menu, recipes, and inventory management practices accordingly.

By following these steps, you will be able to calculate your food cost accurately and make informed decisions about pricing and menu offerings for your food business. Good luck!

How to Strengthen Your Relationship with Your Best Friend

Having a best friend is one of life's greatest joys. However, sometimes, maintaining and strengthening that bond requires a little extra effort. Here are 35 ways to strengthen your relationship with your best friend:

1. Share personal, sentimental items with each other. This can be something as simple as a favorite book, or something more personal like a piece of jewelry.

2. Do something scary together. Stepping out of your comfort zone together can be a bonding experience.

3. Try a bit of space. Spending time apart can help you appreciate each other more when you do spend time together.

4. Create something together. Whether it's a craft project or a business venture, working on a project together can be a fun way to strengthen your bond.

5. Conquer your fears together. Facing and overcoming your fears with your best friend can create a deep sense of trust and closeness.

6. Set aside time for the two of you. Make a point to have regular one-on-one time together, even if it's just grabbing coffee once a week.

7. Listen to each other. Take the time to really hear what your friend is saying and be a supportive sounding board.

8. Utilize technology and social media. Send each other funny memes or Snapchats throughout the day to keep the connection strong.

9. Build trust. Trust is a crucial element of any strong friendship, so work to earn and maintain each other's trust.

10. Make a scrapbook. Collect memories and mementos from your friendship in a scrapbook to look back on and reminisce.

11. Make a video reel. Create a video montage of your friendship to remember all the fun times you've had together.

12. Sleep over at each other's houses. Nothing says BFF like a good old-fashioned sleepover.

13. Plan time to go out and have fun together. Whether it's a night out on the town or a day at the beach, make time to have fun together.

14. Try to end arguments as soon as possible. Don't let grudges and hurt feelings linger. Work to resolve conflicts as soon as they arise.

15. Avoid jealousy. Jealousy can be toxic to friendships, so celebrate each other's successes and don't begrudge each other's accomplishments.

16. Talk about the good and the bad. Be open and honest with each other, sharing both positive and negative experiences and feelings.

17. Don't be exclusive. Cooperate and include other friends and acquaintances in your activities.

18. Get to know each other better. Make an effort to learn more about your friend's interests, hobbies, and goals.

19. Tell each other everything. Share your secrets, your hopes, and your fears with each other.

20. Give each other gifts. Tokens of appreciation and love can go a long way in strengthening your bond.

21. Be there for them. Support your friend through tough times, and be a shoulder to cry on when needed.

22. Help them with the little and the big. Whether it's helping them move or lending an ear after a bad day, be there for your friend.

23. Find a restaurant or cafe you both like and meet there regularly. Developing regular hangout spots can create a sense of stability and familiarity.

24. Go to each other's houses after school as much as possible. Spending time together after school is a great way to strengthen your bond.

25. Don't lie or hold back. Honesty is key in any strong friendship, so don't sugarcoat the truth.

26. Make inside jokes together. Shared laughs and private jokes can create a strong sense of connection.

27. Be available at all times. Show your friend that you're there whenever they need you.

28. Compliment each other. Affirming each other's positive traits and accomplishments can create a sense of mutual respect.

29. Visit each other in person as much as possible. Nothing beats spending time together in person.

30. Find interests you share and build upon them. Cultivate shared interests to strengthen your connection.

31. Have fun together. Laughter and enjoyment are key components of any good friendship.

32. Make plans for the future. Dream together and make goals for your future friendship.

33. Don't let love get in the way. If one of you enters into a romantic relationship, work to maintain your friendship despite any changes.

34. Become family. Develop a sense of kinship between you and your best friend.

35. Don't be negative. Avoid toxic behavior like gossip and negativity, and strive to be a positive force in each other's lives.

By following these tips and actively working to strengthen your bond, you and your best friend can create a deep and lasting friendship that will withstand the test of time. Remember, you are two parts of one whole.

Are you looking to make a DJ mix set using Ableton Live? Well, look no further! Follow these simple steps and you'll be able to create a stunning mix set that'll have your audience grooving all night long.

Step 1: Compile a selection of tracks that you want to use in your DJ mix.

The first step in creating your DJ mix set is to pick out the tracks you want to use. Think carefully about the mood you want to create and what kind of music will get your audience moving.

Step 2: Open Ableton and locate the source folder.

Open Ableton Live and navigate to the source folder where your audio files are stored.

Step 3: Create an additional audio channel.

In Ableton Live, create an additional audio channel by right-clicking in the empty space to the right of a track and selecting "Insert Audio Track."

Step 4: Drag your files from the navigation bar into the audio channels.

Drag your audio files from the navigation bar into the audio channels. You can drag and drop multiple files at once.

Step 5: Double click the first track on your list.

Double click the first track on your list to open it.

Step 6: Zoom in on the first warp marker.

Zoom in on the first warp marker by pressing the "Z" key on your keyboard.

Step 7: Adjust your warp markers.

Adjust your warp markers until the waveform lines up with the grid in Ableton Live.

Step 8: Confirm that everything is synchronized.

Confirm that everything is synchronized by playing the track and watching the waveform as it progresses. Make any adjustments as necessary.

Step 9: Repeat steps 5 - 8 for each one of the tracks in your set.

Repeat steps 5 - 8 for each one of the tracks in your set. Be sure to take your time and carefully synchronize each track.

Step 10: Save your work.

Save your work by clicking "Save" in the upper left corner of the Ableton Live window.

Step 11: Open your set in Ableton Live.

Open your set in Ableton Live and place the tracks in the order you would like to play them.

Step 12: Play the first track.

Play the first track and make sure everything is sounding good.

Step 13: Play the second track.

Play the second track and use the crossfader to smoothly transition between the two tracks.

Step 14: Delete the first track from the session window.

Delete the first track from the session window to make room for the rest of your set.

Step 15: Play the third track.

Play the third track and continue to mix and transition between tracks using the crossfader.

Step 16: Repeat steps 4 - 6 for the rest of your set.

Repeat steps 4 - 6 for the rest of your set, making sure each track seamlessly transitions into the next.

Step 17: Open your Ableton project file.

Open your Ableton project file and copy the first track from the session window.

Step 18: Arrange your tracks.

Arrange your tracks by dragging and dropping them into the arrangement window. This will allow you to see the entire timeline of your mix set.

Step 19: Repeat these steps until all your tracks are placed in the arrangement window.

Repeat these steps until all your tracks are placed in the arrangement window. You can adjust the length of each track by dragging the beginning or end of the waveform.

Step 20: Mix your tracks.

Mix your tracks by adjusting the levels of each track and using the EQ to make sure everything sounds balanced.

Step 21: Fade your tracks.

Fade your tracks by using the volume faders and crossfader to smoothly transition between tracks.

Step 22: Repeat these actions for each transition for your set.

Repeat these actions for each transition in your set. Take your time and make sure each transition is smooth and seamless.

Step 23: Prepare your mix for export.

Prepare your mix for export by adjusting the levels and EQ one last time, then select "Export Audio/Video" from the file menu. Choose your export settings and export your mix set.

Congratulations! You've just created a DJ mix set using Ableton Live that's sure to get your audience moving. Have fun and enjoy the music!

Airbrushing is a great way to enhance the appearance of your photos and images. If you're using Adobe CS4, the process is fairly simple, but can provide wonderful results. This wikiHow article will show you how to airbrush your photos in Adobe CS4.

Part 1: Preparing the Image

1. Open the photo or image you want to edit in your Photoshop CS4. To do this, open Photoshop and then select "File" > "Open". Choose the image that you want to edit and click "Open".

2. Duplicate the layer by selecting the layer you want to duplicate and pressing “Ctrl + J” on your keyboard.

3. Apply a blur filter on your duplicated layer by going to "Filter" > "Blur" > "Surface Blur". A window will appear with the blur filter settings. Adjust the settings to your desired level of blurriness. Click "OK" when you're done.

4. Create a new layer within the folder or the group. To do this, click on the folder or group you want to add the layer to, then click "Layer" > "New".

5. Fill the layer with a neutral color such as gray by going to "Edit" > "Fill". A new window will appear. Select "Gray" as the color and click "OK".

6. After filling the layer with a gray color, add some texture to the filled color. To do this, apply a 2% amount of noise on the image by going to "Filter" > "Noise" > "Add Noise". Select 2% as the amount of noise and click "OK".

7. Blend the texture by applying a hint of blur on the layer. To do this, set the blur settings to a 1% amount of pixels. Go to "Filter" > "Blur" > "Gaussian Blur". Adjust the settings to your desired level of blur and click "OK".

8. Add a layer mask on the folder or the group by clicking on the folder or group and selecting "Layer" > "Layer Mask" > "Reveal All".

Part 2: Airbrushing

1. Use the airbrush tool to start making your edits. To fully utilize the brush tool, keep the following in mind:

- For a smaller brush, click on the bracket key “[]” on your keyboard.
- Do a little blending with the brush tool to make sure your edits look natural.

2. Wait for the Hue/Saturation window to appear by selecting "Layer" > "New Adjustment Layer" > "Hue/Saturation".

3. Go to "Image" > "Apply Image" for a more realistic effect on the adjusted color. A new window will appear.

4. On the "Apply Image" window, find a color channel that you think presents a clearer skin on the image. Adjust the settings to your desired level of color and click "OK".

5. When you're happy with your edits, save your image by going to "File" > "Save" or "Save As". Choose the location where you want to save the image and select the file format (e.g. JPEG or PNG). Click "Save".

6. Finally, you’re done! Congratulations on airbrushing your image in Adobe CS4!

Write down a few of the things that you like to do for fun. Next, write down a few items that you'd use for that hobby. Choose the ones you really like from that list, and add them to your wish list. Here are some ideas to get you started:


If you like to paint or draw, you might need some new graphite pencils, paintbrushes, or paints. If you do oil painting, you might also need some linseed oil or turpentine. Be creative!
If you like to show support for your favorite sports team, don't limit yourself to just jerseys, sweatshirts, and hats with your favorite team's logo. A sports game is a great way of showing support. It can also be a great experience.
If you like music, why not think about your favorite bands? Are there any new albums that came out or that you don't have? What about posters or shirts?
If you are interested in manga or comic books, see if any new volumes have been added to your favorite series. If you like anime, see what new action figures you might have missed out on.
Click here for more ideas on specific gifts.;
, Did you recently see a musical that you really liked? The musical might not be playing anymore by the time your birthday rolls around, but there could be another one that you might enjoy. Visit the theater company's website and see what is coming up that interests you. Tickets to performances, such as operas, plays, and musicals, make great, memorable gifts.


If you don't like theater, think about other things you have enjoyed. This could be a sports game, a concert, or even a theme park. Click here for more gift ideas based on experiences.

, Sometimes, figuring out what you need is easier than deciding what you want. Think about the past few months. Ask yourself if there was something you really needed at one point but didn't have. Here are some things to get you started:


If you cook a lot, you might find that some of your pots, pans, and other utensils need replacing or updating. You could ask for a new set or blender. If all your cooking gear is in top condition, consider some exotic spices instead. If you have a green thumb, you might like a grow-your-own herb kit. You'll get some pots, soil, and a few popular cooking herbs, such as basil, thyme, and mint.
If you play a sport or music, see if your gear needs updating or replacing. Equipment can be expensive, and a birthday is a great chance to get an upgrade.
If your birthday is close to winter, it might be a good idea to check and see if your winter clothes still fit. If they don't, you could ask for a new jacket or scarf.

, Do you have a favorite store that you like to shop in? Visit their website and see if there is anything new that came out since the last time you visited. Sometimes, walking through a store, thumbing through a catalog, or surfing the web may give you some ideas.


If you have a free weekend, try visiting your local mall. Be sure to take note of anything that interests you.

, Chances are, you might be interested in more than one thing, such as drawing, painting, and knitting. You might also find yourself wanting anything and everything related to your craft. This could easily get overwhelming. To keep this from happening, you could always ask for a set or kit. They usually contain everything you need to complete a project or two. This would also make it easier on your friends and family when they go gift-shopping; they won't have to worry about getting the right supplies or missing an important one. Here are some ideas to get you started:


If you like beading, you might like a beading set. Most will have everything you need to create a necklace, a pair of earrings, and a bracelet. They will come with beading wire, clasps and crimps, and beads. You might also be interested in getting some polymer clay so that you can make your own beads.
If you are into DIY, you might like a soap or candle-making kit. You could also ask for supplies for a basic DIY project, such as chalkboard paint, mason jars, burlap, twine, and paint brushes.
If you like to draw, you could ask for a set of graphite or charcoal pencils, a sketchbook, and a how-to-draw book. These books come on just about every subject, from people to plants and trees to animals. Some even focus on specific animals, such as birds, cats, dogs, or horses. If you like fantasy creatures, then there are books on how to draw mermaids, fairies, elves, and dragons. There are even books on how to draw anime.
If you like to paint, consider an art set. Many arts and crafts stores sell sets that come in wooden or metal cases. You can get them with artist-grade acrylic, oil, or watercolor paints. Some sets may also including a how-to-paint book, some art paper, or a canvas.
If you like knitting or crochet, you don't have to limit yourself to just plain old yarn. You would treat yourself to some fancier, more expensive yarn with different fibers and textures. There are also many pattern books available that you might like as well.

, Things like computers, phones, and tablets are always updating, and what is new one year will be old and outdated the next. Accessories, such as cases and earphones, however, won't get old as fast and could last you much longer. Here are some ideas:

, Jewelry can be expensive, but it doesn't have to be. You can get many beautiful, handmade pieces on artist websites, such as Etsy, and at craft fairs. Look through your jewelry collection and see if there anything you wish you to complete an outfit, such as a brooch, bracelet, or necklace. If you already have a lot of jewelry but nothing to keep it in, you could ask for a jewelry box instead.

Belts and wallets can make great gifts. If you get them in leather, you might be able to get them customized; some leather can be stamped with a design or letters.

, Just be sure to list your favorite colors, shades, and scents as these can be personal. Like jewelry, makeup does not take up a lot of space, and it can be used almost very day. Here are some ideas to get you started:

, If you have a favorite author or genre, ask for the latest book in a series. The New York Times bestseller list can help you find the most popular books in a variety of genres. Let the giver know what your tastes. Maybe he or she has read something you would enjoy. Here are some more ideas:

, These gifts are often more personal and special than store-bought gifts. The person might even be flattered that you think their talents are gift-worthy. Handmade gifts are both unique and special, and sure to stand out. Here are some things you could ask for:

If one of your friends likes to make soaps or candles, see if they could make you a set.

, Sometimes, your favorite store may not be carrying something that you like at the moment. A gift card will allow you to set money aside to spend at that store when you do find something that you like.

, If the budget is large, you might ask to go on a trip to a place you’ve never been to. If the budget is more limited, ask to spend a day with the giver. It can be as simple as going out to a meal or to a museum in your town. Here's some more ideas:

Go to the park. It can be as simple as your local neighborhood park. It can also be a state or national park.

Bungee jumping
Caving
Hiking or backpacking
Horseback riding
Kayaking
Rock climbing
Scuba diving

If you don't like pedicures, you might like a massage or a facial with a mud mask. Be sure to schedule your appointment in advance, as some of the more popular spa centers get their spots filled up fast.

, Many businesses offer gift certificates to learn a new skill, such as dancing, martial arts, painting, or woodworking. You can also see if you can spend the day learning a special skill from a family member. Your grandmother may be delighted to teach you how to bake a cake or cook a favorite dish. And the best part is that you can eat what you cook after you are done. Here are some more ideas:


If you like beading, cake decorating, crochet, knitting, or painting, visit an arts and crafts store. Many of them offer classes in these areas.

, They are great gifts for those who enjoy art or history. Many museums are themed, and focus on a certain type of history (such as ancient Egyptian or Medieval) or a certain type of art (such as Asian or French Impressionist). Think about what interests you, and see if there is a museum that features your interests.


If history or art aren't your think, you might be interested in a sports or music hall of fame. You might also enjoy visiting a wax museum or a museum that focuses on technology and innovation.

, Most of the time, you only have to pay an entrance fee, and can spend as much time as you want. Some zoos and aquariums may allow you to get up close and personal with some of the animals for an additional fee. If you are interested in this, visit your local zoo or aquarium's website, and see if this is an option.

, Sometimes, the memories of an event can outlast any physical gift. Many theaters and concert halls may also have gift shops that sell posters, CDs, and shirts that you can buy to help you remember the experience even more.


See if your favorite band is playing close to you, and ask for a ticket to their concert. You can make the experience even more special by asking for a VIP pass, so that you can meet your favorite band members and have them sign items for you.
If you like classical music, you might be interested in attending a concert with a live orchestra.
If you like singing and dancing, you might enjoy watching a musical. If you like performing arts, but without the singing or dancing, try a play instead.

, Keep in mind, however, that if this convention is out of town, and if you will be staying there overnight, you may need a place to stay there. Many hotels that host the conventions offer special prices on rooms.


If anime or comic books aren't your think, you might be interested in a Renaissance Faire instead. They are usually open only on weekends, so you do not need to stay there overnight. They are a great way to immerse yourself in history and fantasy.
If you have a favorite author or illustrator, see if he or she is holding a reading or signing in your area. Not only will you be able to meet someone you adore, but you may also walk away with an autograph.

, An experience doesn't always have to be active. It can also be as simple as enjoying good food with friends and family. Choose a restaurant that you love, or that you have always wanted to go to.

, Sometimes, the gift of giving may feel more rewarding than the gift of receiving. Think about some things that you are passionate about, and try to find an organization that supports that. Here are some ideas:


Animals and nature
The homeless
Disaster relief
Education

, If you can't decide between a few items, make a pro and con list. Write down the good things and the bad things about each item on your list. Choose the item that has the most advantages and the fewest disadvantages. For example, a jacket may not be very exciting, but you could wear it with many different outfits; it may also keep you warm in the wintertime.

, It might be school, a job, sports, or something else entirely. If playing sports are the most important thing for you, new equipment might be more useful than that new video game—which you may not have the time to play between practices.

, Sometimes, what you want right now may not be what you will want (or use) later on down the road. If you can't decide between a few items, try to imagine your life without each of those items a few months later. Choose the item that you will continue to use, or still be interested in, over those whose novelty will fade.


You can also try imagining what it would be like not to get one of these gifts. Choose the one you'd be most upset over not getting.

, Not everyone might be able to spend a lot of money on a gift. If you want something very expensive, try asking the person for a budget before posting your wish list. If you ask for something that the person is unable to afford, he or she might feel embarrassed. Here are some other options:


If you are too shy to ask for a budget, put a few expensive and inexpensive things on your wish list. This will let people buy what is within their budget.
Ask for a group gift. This will allow everyone in your family or circle of friends to chip in when the time comes to buy that expensive gift.
Ask for a gift that doubles up for another occasion. For example, if your birthday is in the winter, you could ask for a gift that is both a birthday and Christmas present
Offer to pay for part of it yourself. The combination of your money and theirs may allow you to get something expensive that you really want.

, If you cannot decide between two or three things, ask someone else to choose for you. Give that person the list, and tell him or her to choose one item from that list. Some people may even prefer being able to choose a gift for you.

, If you try to meet other people's expectations, you will end up getting stressed. You may also end up not getting something that you really wanted.If the trip to the beach is what would really make you happy, let your family know. You do not have to choose an expensive gift for your birthday, just because all your friends do on theirs.

Playing the marimba can be an enjoyable and unique experience. While you may be able to purchase pre-made mallets, making your own can allow you to customize the sound and feel of your instrument. In this article, we will show you how to make marimba mallets by cutting the dowels to the right length, preparing the ball knob, wrapping the mallet, and finishing it off for use.

Part 1: Cutting the Dowels

1. Select a wooden dowel of your preferred diameter. The standard diameter for a marimba mallet is around ⅜ of an inch.
​
2. Use a saw to cut the dowel into a length of about 14-17 inches. This length is ideal for most mallets. Ensure the length is precise by measuring it with a ruler or measuring tape.

Part 2: Preparing the Ball Knob

1. Obtain a ball knob that can fit the size of your dowel. The knob should be soft enough to work with – a rubber knob is ideal.

2. Use a pair of scissors or a knife to shave the inside of the ball knob until the hole is large enough to accommodate your dowel snugly.​ 

3. Insert the dowel into the hole, ensuring it slides about half an inch into the ball knob.

Part 3: Wrapping the Mallet

1. With a few rubber bands, wrap the mallet tightly near one end, ensuring the ball knob and dowel are secure.
​
2. Tie a clove hitch around the bottom of the rubber core, about one inch from the bottom of the mallet.

3. Holding the mallet in your hand near the clove hitch, start wrapping the mallet. Make the first wrap once around the shaft, over the clove hitch. 

4. Turn the mallet around the shaft and make another wrap, perpendicular or 90 degrees to the first one.

5. Repeat step 4 until you reach the top of the ball knob.

6. Once you have reached the top, you should have a little nipple where the lines overlap. Still holding the mallet in your hand near the top, wind it once more around the shaft, ensuring it goes over the clove hitch. Overlap the wrap with the nipple at an angle of approximately 45 degrees.

7. Turn the mallet around the shaft and make another X following the previous two steps.

8. Continue wrapping the mallet in the same way. Keep count of the number of wraps you make.

9. When you have made the number of wraps you want, there should be a little hole at the top. Thread the needle and stitch the top of the mallet, ensuring you keep it tight.

10. Repeat the entire pattern, tightly wrapping the mallet until you reach the bottom of the rubber core. Leave at least 3 inches of slack at the beginning of the wrapping process for making the bottom ring.

11. With the little slack remaining, make a bottom ring by stitching around the mallet base using the same "donut" stitching technique.

12. Repeat all the steps above to make an identical second marimba mallet.

Part 4: Finishing the Mallet

1. After completing the first mallet, sand it down to an exact length. Use sandpaper to smooth the bottom of the dowel if you plan to use it in a 4 mallet grip. 

2. Repeat the sanding process on the second mallet and ensure that both mallets are of equal length.

3. Your custom-made marimba mallets are now ready for use!

Pick your favorite...or mix 'n' match! (Since Los Angeles, Orange County and San Diego more or less flow together, hot vacation spots from all these places are included here.);

, You'll find Malibu Beaches (Malibu alone has 27 miles (43 km) of coastline!), Santa Monica Beach (with Palisades Park amusement area on the pier!), Venice Beach (home of the wild and weird); Manhattan and Hermosa Beaches (LA's beach volleyball mecca); Redondo Beach (including marina and pier lined with shops & restaurants); Huntington Beach (surfing and active sports headquarters); Newport Beach (has Balboa Fun Zone and harbor tours); Laguna Beach (art colony and famous because of MTV); Doheny Beach at Dana Point (good beach tent camping); San Onofre Beach (another famous surfing spot); Mission Beach (San Diego; with Belmont Amusement Park); Pacific Beach (San Diego) and Imperial Beach (closest beach to Mexico).
