You can get one free credit report from every year from Equifax, Experian, and Transunion.


If there are issues or errors, it will generally take about 2-3 months to make contact with the sources of any discrepancies and either remove or correct the errors. Disputes must be investigated within 30 days, but errors are not always resolved on the first try. Disputes must be removed before applying for a loan, and they do not go away automatically.;
, Even if these accounts need to be paid before closing, it is best to arrange to pay them through escrow. Paying old debts before applying for a loan makes the accounts "active" again, and can potentially decrease your credit score. Old debts have less influence on your credit score than newer debts and delinquencies.

,,,,


The current market value minus your mortgage debt equals the amount of your home equity.

,, Cash out refinances typically need to have an LTV of 85% or less. Don't forget to include closing costs in the total loan amount, unless you will bring cash to closing. If you will be taking a home equity loan or line of credit instead of refinancing the entire mortgage, there will be two loans and this ratio will be called the "Combined Loan to Value" or CLTV.

,, Discuss options with your loan officer and apply for the type of loan that is best for your current financial situation.


The three methods for accessing equity are: refinancing your existing mortgage and taking cash out, applying for a home equity loan, or opening a line of credit based on equity.

,,,, Funds are accessed as needed, and the balance is paid monthly.


This option is best suited for those with long-term projects or other expenses that will evolve over time.

, Be aware that they cannot give you an accurate quote without pulling your credit report, and a fairly accurate estimate of your home value.

,, Take into consideration the loan officer's level of customer service and speed in processing your loan when making your decision. If you take more than one day to shop lenders, the rates will be changing on a daily basis, and sometimes more than once per day, so while one lender may seem less expensive, it may be due to rates declining.

, They should have already collected the majority of your paperwork when determining whether or not you qualify.

, Discuss locking your interest rate with your loan officer. Review all disclosures and submit additional paperwork as requested as soon as possible to keep your file moving forward. Your loan is not officially approved until it funds, so stay on top of any requests!

Having at least a bachelor's degree is a requirement. This is not a requirement for the job, but rather for the work visa. Without a work visa (or a spouse visa if you are married to a Japanese citizen) you cannot legally work in Japan. This is an immigration law. Without a bachelor's degree, Japan will not issue you a work visa. You don't want to break the law in Japan. If you are caught working without a visa you will be detained and deported. Your bachelor's degree doesn't need to be in English or teaching, but it might be more useful. Any bachelor's degree will do.;
, If you do want to work in Japan then you will need a lot of money. It is recommended to bring at least $2,000 with you to help you get started before you get your first paycheck. In addition, you will need to buy suits for your job. Most schools will require you to wear a suit, but some let you take off the suit jacket in the classroom, especially during summer time. You should have at least 3 nice suits. Also, you will have to pay for your plane tickets. Depending on your interview location, you may need to fly there (even within your own country). You also need to pay for your own flight to Japan.

, In other words, no arrests. The government will not issue a visa to someone who has committed a crime. Small crimes several years ago may be overlooked, but anything in the past 5 years is almost a guarantee than your visa will be denied.

, There are hundreds of English schools in Japan. Most of them are private and they are usually called "Eikaiwa" which means "English Conversation". These schools generally offer a good work environment and are very easy to join. They also help you with getting your life set up in Japan. The pay is also good for an entry level job.


Use the internet and read about the different types of schools. There are maybe 4 very famous ones with branch schools all across the country, but hundreds of smaller ones too. Start by making a list of the famous schools in Japan. Or, if there is a particular city that you want to go to, try looking for schools in that city.
Read former teachers' experiences on the internet. Many teachers will write about their experiences while working at their schools. This is a good way to see the pros and cons or each place.
Visit the school's website directly. They will provide a lot of information on salaries, types of lessons, housing, responsibilities, and so on.
Read students' comments. If you can read Japanese, it is a very good idea to read student's comments about the school they went to. This will give you the best information on that company's atmosphere. Students' comments usually differ greatly from teachers' comments because they see the school from a different perspective. Reading both will help you choose the school that's right for you.

, Your work life is only a part of your life in Japan. You should read up on Japanese culture and mannerisms. Read people's personal experiences rather than books. Books often contain stereotypical or outdated information. Real people's experiences will give you better insight on life in Japan. Does this type of life suit you? Remember, you will be working in a Japanese work environment (depending on the school), and probably all of your students will be Japanese, so it's essential to understand their culture.

, You will probably have a short English test at the interview. It will include conjugating verbs in different tenses (for example: Past Perfect), and also a spelling section. It is highly recommended to find a list of commonly misspelled words and to practice conjugating irregular verbs, even if your native language is English.

, You don't need it for your job, but it's useful for reading student's names and also for using the computers. You will likely need it for you life in Japan, especially if you don't live in a big city.

, In other words, you must live in Japan and work at that company for at least 1 year. You will have Golden Week, Obon, and New Year's vacation which you can use to visit your family in your home-town. Other than that, be prepared to be separated from your family and your friends for at least 1 year.
Do not break your contract. It is not easy for a company to scout new teachers, do the paperwork, and train them. Between the time you leave and the time a new teacher comes, your school will be in a lot of trouble. They will have to send a substitute or emergency teacher, which is very expensive. If you break your contract the company can hold you accountable for those expenses and will charge you, even if you move back to your home country.
Furthermore, the students need a teacher who will be there for them. If you leave suddenly then your students' motivation will drop, and they don't deserve that. Are you ready to make a minimum 1-year commitment?

, Find a suitable interview location and time for you. Follow the school's instructions on the website and apply.


You might have to write an essay about why you want to work and live in Japan. Follow the guidelines set by the company. Following guidelines is not only important in these schools, but in all of Japan. You should write about how you love Japan and love teaching. Highlight your strengths in the essay as well.

These schools are looking for enthusiastic teachers, so you might want to include words like "deep interest" "overwhelming passion" "intellectually stimulating" and so on. For example "I have held a deep interest in Japan and teaching since I was in junior high school. In our history class, we learned how to write our name in katakana and it really piqued my curiosity in the culture. Furthermore, I have an overwhelming passion for learning and teaching and hope to pursue it in my future." Using these words will let the employer know more about your personality.


Your essay should show your personality, but it should also reflect your English ability. You will probably be required to teach students from beginners to advanced. Using advanced vocabulary and expressions will make your essay stand out. For example, instead of saying "I have always wanted to be a teacher." say "I have always had my heart set on a teaching career."
Do not use slang, which can be considered unprofessional. Being professional is extremely important and these schools pride themselves on that image. Show that you are a well educated, determined, professional, and competent individual with a lot of energy and passion.

, This is pretty simple. If you don't know how to do this, there are some great articles about writing resumes on wikiHow.

, A sure way to get your application denied is to have it be riddled with spelling and grammar mistakes. Proofread it several times and have someone else read it too. If you really aren't sure about some of the grammar, read about grammar rules on the internet. Chances are, you will be doing this anyway at your future job for more advanced grammar so you can explain it clearly to your students.

, You should make a 50-minute lesson plan about what type of lesson you want to teach. If you are accepted for the interview, you will choose any 5 minutes of your plan that you want to demonstrate to the interviewers. Make your plan for a beginner class (intermediate may be acceptable). Make it fun and engaging. The only speaking you should do is giving instructions. Make the plan so the students do the speaking or activity amongst themselves. Remember, you are applying for a job teaching English conversation, so make the students practice conversation. Give them some target vocabulary, a grammar point, and a situation to work with.

,, Most applications are accepted, but the interview is where most people are weeded out. Your interview will likely be in a hotel, so book a room there. The interview may be in two stages, with each stage on different days. If you pass the first stage, then the next stage will be the next day. Book at least 2 nights in the hotel.

, Just as there is no excuse for being late to work, there is never any excuse for being late to an interview. Arrange your travel plans accordingly.

,


Bring two suits, nice shoes, a nice pen, a pad of paper for notes, and any props or materials you want to use for your lesson. If you have print outs, print them in color. If you are using flash cards, laminate them. Make it as professional as possible. Your lesson demonstration is only 5 minutes, but the amount of work you put into preparing will impress your interviewers. Never start your demo lesson without some pictures or props. Iron your suits and polish your shoes.
Don't bring perfume, extra makeup (foundation only is okay) more than 1 pair of earrings, more than 1 ring, any other flashy or colorful accessories. While people in Japan do wear many accessories, they do not wear them in the office. Excessive makeup like eyeliner and eyeshadow is frowned upon. Painted nails are a big no-no. (Clear polish is okay though). These things are unprofessional and won't even be allowed at your school if you are hired.
If you are a woman, wear stockings and closed toed heeled shoes. Do not wear ballet shoes. No bright colors (pink, red, yellow, orange) and also do not wear all black. Schools want to show a professional, but "bright" friendly image. Think about that before you go.
If you are man, shave your beard or trim it very short. It is relatively rare of men in Japan to grow beards, especially businessmen. If they do have one, it is always neatly trimmed. This will be a requirement at the school if you are hired.
Hide any tattoos. Your school will not hire you if you display a tattoo. Some schools are okay if you have one, but you must keep it hidden and never tell students. The students "might" not care, but if they tell the staff at your school then you may have trouble.

, This is important for your future job and most events in Japan. Always arrive 10-15 minutes early.

, Japanese is usually not required for this job. Also, at your school you will likely be prohibited from speaking Japanese to the students or even in front of students. Speaking Japanese to an interviewer or during your demo lesson is a good way to fail your interview. Again, the schools do not want you to speak Japanese at the school.

, Take notes and listen carefully. Ask questions to show your interest and show that you are actively listening.

, You should have already decided which 5-minute part you want to demonstrate. There will be several interviewers and many interviewees. The other interviewees will be your students. And you will be their students when it is their turn. You will likely have more than 1 interviewer watching your lesson. Be prepared for it. Take a deep breath and drink some water.

,


Smile a lot. This is a huge plus. Smile and make your students smile. Happy students are students who will want to continue studying and will love to come to your class. So, smile.
Give instructions clearly, slowly, and simply. Speak only when necessary.
Use gestures. Go overboard. Be funny. Schools want a teacher who can explain things without using words and also someone who can hold students' attention. Using gestures and smiling a lot will also help you forget about your nervousness. Enjoy yourself and your students will enjoy it too. And so will your interviewer.
Teach them something. Even if they have "free conversation", teach them more advanced phrases. For example, if the topic is "Talk about a trip you took" and a student (interviewee) says "It was great.", teach them "it was fantastic" or "it was out of this world". Teach them something, but make sure they talk a lot and practice what you taught them. You can even make them repeat once or twice.
Don't get upset with your students. Chances are, one of your students (another interviewee) will try to make your demo lesson harder by asking an unrelated question or by not following instructions. Don't worry. Just smile, answer it if you can, and continue the lesson. If you cannot answer their question, do not worry! Just say "That's a very good question (student's name). Let's talk about it together after the lesson. Let's continue now." In your school, you will have student's like this. Knowing how to handle them and control the lesson is essential for a teacher. Promise to help them, but at a later time.
Don't talk too much. Don't lecture. You are teaching English conversation. You want your student's to talk.
Don't make another interviewee's demo lesson hard. Be a good student. Do exactly as you are told. Interfering with another person's demo lesson will look unprofessional.

, You will either be invited to a second interview, or you won't.

, It will likely be only 1 interviewer and you. They will ask you the typical interview questions. Be prepared with your answers.

, You cannot prepare for the second demo lesson. It is sprung on you without any warning. It will probably be a kid's lesson. The interviewer may show you a book, open to a page, and tell you "You have 1 minute to prepare and then 3 minutes to teach me something from this page. Also, I am a 5-year-old." The interviewer will leave the room and you have a short time to look at that page and decide what / how you want to teach. Let's imagine there are zoo animals on the page.

, The interviewer will return but they have the mindset of a 5-year-old. They won't act up, but they will act sometimes like they don't understand you. Do what you need to to teach them something and make it fun. Even be funny if you have to. If your page is zoo animals, make animal noises and then say the animal's name. Use gestures too. Make your arm an elephant's nose. Say "together" and do it with your student and then repeat the animal's name. This may be weird for you, but it's fun for a 5-year-old. Also, they are unlikely to forget the vocabulary you taught them! You will have to be able to teach lessons on the fly sometimes, so being able to prepare in a short time is essential.

, Be specific. Big city, small city, countryside, ocean, mountains, and so on. Also tell them if you want to teach kids or adults. Tell them exactly what you want. If they want to hire you then they will find a good place for you, even if it takes a few months.

, Wait for a phone call.

, If you were an energetic and friendly teacher who put a lot of effort into preparing a demo lesson and could also teach a fun lesson off the cuff, then you should get a job as an English teacher in Japan.

, Ask any questions that you may have.


They'll send you a contract. Read it very carefully. Very carefully. Remember, it is a legal agreement. Don't break it and don't take it lightly.

,, Some medications are illegal in Japan.

, Bring only the bare necessities. You can buy things in Japan when you arrive or have your family send them to you later. Your apartment will be small and the training center will be small too. Just bring suits, casual clothes, and things for hygiene. Maybe a book for studying Japanese.

, Go with your trainer and new group to your training center. You may receive training there for a while. Get friendly with your fellow trainees.


You'll receive a few days of training. Do not take it lightly. It can be fun, but it's long. You will receive homework and assignments. They will help you learn how to do your job for the next year. Do not skip them. Do everything meticulously. It is possible to get kicked out of training and never sent to your branch school. Again, if you don't take training seriously, the company can send you back home.

,

How to Be an Amazing Person: Know the Proven Benefits of Being Generous

Being an amazing person means being kind, generous, and thoughtful towards others. Generosity is an important trait that comes with many proven benefits. It can improve your overall sense of well-being, increase your happiness, boost your immune system, and even lower your blood pressure!

If you're looking to become an amazing person, one of the best places to start is with small acts of generosity. This can mean anything from donating money to a good cause, volunteering your time, or simply offering a helping hand to those in need.

Before you start, take stock of all the things you're grateful for in your life. This will help you stay positive and focused on the good things in your life, and give you a sense of perspective when you're feeling down.

Supporting causes you believe in is also a great way to practice generosity. Whether it's by donating money, volunteering your time, or spreading awareness, your support can make a real difference in the lives of those who need it most.

If you're more motivated by people than causes, investing in a person you believe in can also be a great way to practice generosity. This could mean being a mentor, offering your support and guidance to someone who needs it, or simply being a good listener when someone needs to talk.

Volunteering with those less fortunate than you is another great way to practice generosity. Whether it's volunteering at a homeless shelter, a food bank, or a youth center, your time and energy can make a huge difference in the lives of those you serve.

It's also important to take note of the times when you're unkind to others. By recognizing when and why you're unkind, you can work to disarm the trap of your unkindness and become a more compassionate and caring person.

Additionally, accepting and talking through your negative feelings can help you become a more resilient and compassionate person. Remember that all humans have negative thoughts and feelings, and that it's okay to acknowledge and work through them.

To be an amazing person, it's also important to take care of your body. This includes eating a healthy diet, staying hydrated, getting plenty of rest and exercise, and avoiding unhealthy habits like smoking and excessive drinking.

Shaping your world view to focus on positivity and kindness can also help you become an amazing person. This means surrounding yourself with positive influences, staying informed about current events, and practicing empathy and compassion in your daily life.

Another key trait of amazing people is their ability to listen. Whether it's listening to a friend who needs to vent, or listening to the concerns of an entire community, being a good listener is an important skill that can help you forge deeper connections with those around you.

Discipline is also an important trait for amazing people. This means setting goals, staying focused, and sticking to your commitments, even when things get tough.

To develop your discipline, it's important to limit temptation and distractions, take breaks to restore your willpower, and actively exercise your discipline on a daily basis.

Finally, committing to your goals and planning for success are key components of being an amazing person. By accentuating the positive, staying focused, and staying true to your values, you can become the best version of yourself and lead a truly amazing life.

Living in a haunted house can be a thrilling, yet daunting experience. However, with the right approach, it is possible to coexist peacefully with the resident ghost(s). In this article, we will guide you through the process of living in a haunted house, from selecting a guard dog to communicating with the ghost. 

Part 1: Moving In
1. Take a well-built guard dog like a German shepherd with you into the house. A dog can provide a sense of security and protection from any potential supernatural threats. 
2. Before moving anything into the house, walk through the entire place. Get a sense of the space and try to identify any areas that may be particularly active with supernatural activity. 
3. Remember: before moving all of your stuff into the place, move in as few items as possible at first. This will allow you to adjust to the space without being overwhelmed. 
4. Keep in mind while moving furniture or other objects in, be alert for any feelings you may have about where an item should be placed. Trust your instincts and try to place objects in areas that feel "right". 
5. Try to relate to the ghost by talking about what life may have been like in the house (if it is an old house), and try to do something former residents would have done in the house while they lived there, like eating some meals in a formal dining room, or listening to period music from the time the house was built. 

Part 2: Communication
1. Try to talk about big events that would have happened while past owners would have been living there. This can help you connect with the history of the house and the people who have lived there before you. 
2. Avoid the area the ghost is in, if (s)he is in one particular place. If there is a specific place that the ghost seems to be associated with, try to avoid that area as much as possible, especially at night. 
3. Try to spend a night in the place. This can help you get a sense of what it's like living with a ghost and help you adjust to any noises or other phenomena that may occur. 
4. Accept the ghost. Instead of trying to get rid of the ghost, try to accept its presence as a part of the house's history. 
5. Talk to the ghost. If you feel comfortable, try talking to the ghost in a respectful manner. You can introduce yourself, ask questions, or simply acknowledge its presence. 

Part 3: Dealing with Scary Behavior
1. Remember if the ghost does something scary, let it know you are upset, and that if such behavior continues it will not be tolerated. It's important to set boundaries with the ghost and let it know what behavior is acceptable. 
2. If you are feeling overwhelmed or scared, take a break and leave the house for a while. It's important to prioritize your own well-being and safety. 
3. Seek support from others who have had similar experiences. Talking to others who have lived in haunted houses can be helpful in processing your own experiences and learning new coping mechanisms. 

Living in a haunted house can be a challenging and exciting experience. By following these tips, you can coexist peacefully with the resident ghost(s) and embrace the history and character of your new home.

How to Become a Physical Design Engineer

Physical design engineering is a fascinating career path that involves designing microchips and circuits to be used in a variety of electronic devices. If you're considering a career in physical design engineering, here are some steps you can take to start your journey towards becoming a physical design engineer.

1. Research the field of physical design engineering to learn more about it to see if it is the right career for you.

The first step to becoming a physical design engineer is researching the field. You need to have a passion for electronic devices, computer engineering, and mathematics. Learn a bit about the duties and responsibilities involved in the job and assess whether or not it's something you'd like to do long-term.

2. Evaluate your strengths and weaknesses relative to your present knowledge in the field and your level of education.

Try to assess your skills in math, science, and computer engineering. These are the essential skills you'll need to succeed in the field of physical design engineering. It's also essential to evaluate your educational background and decide if you're at the right level to start the process.

3. Make an initial assessment in determining a future educational program to train as a physical design engineer.

One of the most crucial steps towards becoming a physical design engineer is deciding on your educational path. You should start with your high school classes in algebra, geometry, trigonometry, and calculus as well as science courses covering chemistry and physics.

4. Research educational programs at colleges and universities offering degree programs in physical design engineering if you have already received your high school diploma.

Once you're finished with high school, you want to research different programs at universities and colleges to see what options are available. Decide whether you want to earn a bachelor's degree in engineering, electrical engineering, computer engineering, or microelectronic engineering.

5. Contact several professional engineering associations to get more information about becoming a physical design engineer.

There are several professional engineering associations that can provide you with the necessary information to become a physical design engineer. Some of these associations include the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE) and the Association for Computing Machinery (ACM).

6. Apply for membership to one or more professional engineering associations.

Once you've determined which professional engineering association you want to proceed with, you should also apply for membership to get involved in the community and keep up to date with the latest technologies and trends.

7. Visit the campuses of colleges and universities that offer educational programs focusing on the field of physical design engineering.

It is essential to get an idea of the environment you'll be studying and training in. Plan a visit to different campuses to see if the program offered is right for you. Remember, this is a significant investment, so you want to be sure you're choosing the right program.

8. Narrow your choices down to a select few physical design engineering training programs.

After visiting the colleges and universities, you should narrow down your choices to the top programs you're interested in.

9. Plan out your curriculum to include a focused specialty during your studies.

During your studies, you want to focus on a particular specialty that intrigues you. This could range from microchips to computer hardware or software depending on your interests.

10. Consider an internship program during your college training.

Lastly, throughout your college/training period, you should consider an internship program to gain experience in the field and potentially build a network for the future. An internship will give you the knowledge, skills, and practical experience required to excel as a physical design engineer.

In conclusion, becoming a physical design engineer requires hard work and determination. However, the journey can also be exciting as you get to immerse yourself in technology and be part of the creation process of technological advancements. Follow these steps for a headstart in your career as a physical design engineer.

How to Be a Good Personal Assistant

Being a Personal Assistant (PA) is an important role that requires dedication, efficiency, and great interpersonal and communication skills. A good PA is unflappable under pressure, able to multitask and prioritise effectively and, is the first point of contact for customers and clients. Here are some tips on how to be a good personal assistant:

1. Develop Good Interpersonal Skills:

As a PA, you will have to interact with people from all walks of life, from clients to senior executives. You must have the ability to stay calm and professional, even in high-stress situations. This means being empathetic, understanding and tactful at all times.

2. Develop Good Communication Skills:

You must be an excellent communicator both verbally and in writing. You need to have a pleasant yet professional manner to handle calls, emails, and correspondence with clients, colleagues, and suppliers. This includes good phone etiquette, email etiquette and an understanding of business writing.

3. Develop Good IT Skills:

As a corporate Personal Assistant, you are expected to have advanced technical skills. This includes proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, and PowerPoint); a good understanding of email packages like Microsoft Outlook, Lotus Notes, or Eudora; and a good knowledge of an internet environment, including social media and web applications.

4. Develop Good Internet Skills:

As a PA, you will be expected to carry out complex research tasks online, which requires a good knowledge of web search engines and internet tools. You should be comfortable with online calendars, cloud storage, online messaging services, and instant messaging.

5. Develop an Interest In Office Technology:

You must keep abreast with technological advancements and understand their significance in the day-to-day operations of the office. Understanding and keeping up with technology will greatly improve efficiency and output in your role.

6. Develop the Necessary Skills:

There are many aspects to being a good PA, including managing the boss’s email and responding on their behalf. Delegating work on the boss's behalf, managing their electronic diary, taking dictation, and preparing papers for meetings. You will also be responsible for booking, managing, and minute-taking in meetings, managing events, making complicated travel arrangements and preparing complex itineraries. You must be able to manage budgets, attend events or meetings as the boss’s representative, and conduct internet research. Other important skills necessary include preparing presentations, writing correspondence, reports, newsletters and executive summaries, updating intranets and websites, maintaining effective office filing systems, quickly and accurately typing documents, sourcing office equipment, and stationery. You also need to manage projects and supervise staff.

Becoming a good Personal Assistant requires dedication, patience, and willingness to learn. By mastering these skills, you can become a valuable asset to your employer and the organization at large.

Bitcoin is a cryptocurrency that allows users to send money without needing a centralized intermediary, like a bank. One way to store bitcoin is with a paper wallet. A paper wallet is a physical document that contains the private and public keys needed to access a bitcoin wallet.

Creating a Bitcoin Paper Wallet

1. Start with a computer that you are reasonably confident is malware and virus-free. Use a current, well-maintained operating system.

2. Using private browser mode, go to a bitcoin paper wallet creation site. One recommended site is bitaddress.org.

3. Once bitaddress.org loads, turn off the internet on your computer. This ensures that no one can access your keys, even if they try to track your activity.

4. Move your mouse around the screen wildly as instructed by the site. This randomizes your keys and makes them more secure.

5. Keep going until you see that you've created two QR codes and large strings of apparently random numbers and characters as seen in the photo.

6. Store your paper wallet somewhere secure. Consider an alternative physical location for the two copies of the wallet.

Creating a Secure Paper Wallet

1. Follow the instructions in Part 1 above and then click "Paper Wallet."

2. Click the checkmark next to "BIP38 Encrypt" and enter a passphrase. This adds an extra layer of protection to your wallet.

3. Click "Generate."

4. Plug your computer directly into your printer and print your paper wallets.

5. Erase all copies of your paper wallet from your computer and printer before you reconnect to the internet.

Using a Bitcoin Paper Wallet

1. Congratulations! You now have a paper wallet.

2. Before putting any significant amount of money in your wallet, verify that you can successfully decrypt your wallet.

3. Create an online wallet that supports BIP38 encryption.

4. Go to "Settings" then "Addresses" then click "Manage Addresses."

5. Press the QR code scanner to bring up the QR code reader on your webcam.

6. Enter your passphrase.

7. Send Bitcoin to one of your wallets.

8. Verify your funds arrived in your wallet.

9. That's it! Your paper wallet is now in use.

Storing Your Paper Wallet

1. Store the printout of your paper wallet with the private keys in a secure location. Consider having a backup copy.

2. Keep the paper wallet in a waterproof and fireproof container.

3. Do not let anyone else know about your paper wallet or passphrase.

4. Recognize that bitcoin paper wallets are optimal for long-term storage, but not frequent spending.

How to Play Super Mario 64 DS

Super Mario 64 DS is a classic game that has been enjoyed by gamers of all ages since it was first released. From learning how to use the game controls to defeating bosses and unlocking secret levels, this wikiHow guide will walk you through the steps you need to take to play and win Super Mario 64 DS.

1. Learn the controls of the game.
Before you can start playing Super Mario 64 DS, it is essential to familiarize yourself with the game controls. The game has basic controls, such as moving, jumping, and sprinting. However, there are also more advanced moves like wall jumping and backflipping that you will need to learn to progress through the game.

2. Recognize that there are three different control modes in the game.
Super Mario 64 DS has three different control modes that allow you to select the control system that works best for you. The modes include the standard mode, Touch mode, and connection mode.

3. Know that there are four different characters in the game.
In Super Mario 64 DS, you can select among four different characters: Mario, Luigi, Wario, and Yoshi. Each character has unique skills and abilities that make them ideal for specific levels.

4. Recognize what happens when you first turn on your game.
When you first turn on your game, you will see the title screen, which gives you options to start a new game, continue a previously saved game, access the mini-games, or view your records.

5. Start a new save file.
Starting a new save file will allow you to begin playing Super Mario 64 DS from the beginning.

6. Allow the opening cutscene to play.
The opening cutscene is a short cinematic that highlights the storyline of the game. Allow it to play to get an introduction to the game.

7. Catch the rabbit who has the key to unlock the castle.
The first mission of the game is to catch a rabbit that has a key to unlock Peach's Castle. Chase the rabbit, catch it, and take the key.

8. Enter Peach's Castle.
After getting the key, use it to enter Peach's Castle. Peach's Castle is the central hub of the game.

9. Enter the room leading to Bob-Omb Battlefield.
After entering Peach's Castle, locate the painting of Bob-Omb Battlefield. Jump into it to enter the level.

10. Beat the first level of Bob-Omb Battlefield.
The first level of Bob-Omb Battlefield is easy. Complete it and get your first power star.

11. Recognize that you have a lot more choices for what to do now that you have your first star.
Once you have your first star, you gain access to other levels and locations in Peach's Castle.

12. Continue getting stars.
To progress through the game, you need to collect stars. Collect as many as you can by completing different levels and missions.

13. Beat the first Bowser boss battle when you are able to.
Beat the first Bowser boss battle when you collect enough power stars.

14. Know what door that the key he gave you opens.
Bowser gives you a key after defeating him. Use it to unlock the door to the basement of Peach's Castle.

15. Play the next levels that are down in the basement.
After unlocking the basement, play the next levels, including Lethal Lava Land and Shifting Sand Land.

16. Beat the second Bowser boss battle.
After collecting a certain number of power stars, you will face Bowser once again. Beat him to gain access to the final level.

17. Know what this key opens.
Once you defeat Bowser, you will get a key. This key opens the door to the courtyard, one of the secret levels in the game.

18. Play the new levels until you reach 50 stars.
Collect at least 50 stars to unlock new levels and areas in the game.

19. Notice the few new levels that are available to you.
Once you reach 50 stars, notice the few new levels that are available to you, such as Rainbow Ride and Tick Tock Clock.

20. Collect at least 70 stars.
Collect more stars to proceed through Bowser's levels.

21. Beat the final Bowser boss battle.
After collecting enough stars, you will face Bowser one last time. Beat him to complete the game.

22. Watch the final cutscene and the credits.
Watch the final cutscene and the credits to see the ending of the game.

23. Catch rabbits to get keys for the mini-game room.
Catch rabbits to get keys that unlock doors in the mini-game room. Unlock all the doors to unlock all the mini-games.

24. Find the Castle's Secret Stars.
There are many secret stars scattered throughout Peach's Castle. Explore the castle to find them.

25. Use the touch screen map to your advantage.
Use the touch screen map to navigate through levels and find hidden areas.

26. Know what stars look like after you've already gotten them.
After collecting a star, you can see it in the menu screen. It is greyed out if you have already collected it. This helps you keep track of which stars you still need to collect. 

With these steps, you are ready to start playing Super Mario 64 DS. Good luck and have fun!

Creating a website can be daunting, but using a website builder can make the process a lot easier. However, just having a website is not enough to make it successful. To promote your website and increase your rankings, you need to implement a variety of tactics. Below are some steps you can take to boost your website's visibility:

1. Utilize meta tags
With the keywords you have chosen for your site, create a title and description for each web page. A good website builder will generally have these options built-in, and all you will have to do is create a quick and descriptive title tag and brief description and insert it into the required section so that search engines pick up what your website is all about.

2. Create quality content
Ensure that your keywords are used throughout your content, without affecting the quality and readability of the text. Aim to use your keywords mostly at the beginning and end of each paragraph. There are many SEO consultants who advocate this method of on-page optimization.

3. Optimize your images
While images are more to catch the eye rather than for search engine purposes, you can still do certain things to help search engines pick them up. Ensure that the file name of your image is descriptive, it has an alt tag and try to make sure that your images are not too large, as this can slow down your site or web page load time.

4. Submit Your Website to Major Search Engines
Ensure that you submit and verify your website with the three major search engines. Once submitted and verified, your website will show up in search results.

5. Use Article Directories
Articles are a great way to get good rankings and also to get good amounts of traffic. Submitting 3-4 articles a week can have a significant impact on your rankings. Submit your articles to your blog to increase quality and relevant content on your site, then syndicate your articles by submitting them to article directories and other articles resources.

6. Blog Commenting
Blogs are a great way to get a few quick backlinks to your website and for building content on your own website. When commenting on other peoples blogs, don't just drop links for the fun of it, try to add a little comment that relates to the website owner's blog post and drop a link somewhere inside your comment using an html link code so that it doesn't sound too spammy.

7. Utilize Forums
Forums are a good way of getting backlinks to your website and for getting targeted traffic to your site via posting on forums that are relevant to your field. However, forum code and inserting links into forums is slightly different than blogs as you will require a different sort of code to create a backlink known as BB code.

Keep in mind that while these tactics can help boost your website's visibility initially, the key to long-term success is quality content and regular updates. By continually providing value to your audience, you can build a loyal following that will help drive traffic to your website and ensure it stays relevant and successful in the long run.

How to Help the Next Generation of America

The future of America lies in the hands of the next generation. As a responsible citizen, it is up to us to make sure that we prepare them for the challenges ahead. There are many ways to help the next generation of America. You can volunteer or get a job with kids, lead an evening, Saturday or Sunday-school class, make inspirational YouTube videos, or teach them about tragic, wasted lives like crime and drugs. In this article, we will explore some of these ways to help the next generation of America.

1. Consider volunteering or getting a job with kids.

One of the best ways to help the next generation of America is to volunteer your time or get a job with kids. You could work at a local community sports center or at a school. This will give you an opportunity to interact with kids on a regular basis and be a positive influence in their lives.

2. Try to give advice enthusiastically when you can to the children you work with or that are in your family/extended family.

Whenever you have the chance, be sure to give advice to the kids you work with or that are in your family or extended family. But make sure you give it enthusiastically. Kids tend to tune out when they sense that someone is not giving them their full attention.

3. If you are looking for a more highly salaried paying job, consider becoming a teacher or child counselor.

If you have a passion for working with children, then you might want to consider becoming a teacher or child counselor. These professions can be highly rewarding and pay well. You will have the opportunity to help shape the future of America by teaching, mentoring, and counseling the next generation.

4. Lead an evening, Saturday or Sunday-school class or any small group for children or teens, at a youth club, or at a church, temple or another place of worship.

Another great way to help the next generation of America is to lead an evening, Saturday or Sunday-school class or any small group for children or teens. You could do this at a youth club or at a church, temple, or another place of worship. This gives you the opportunity to teach and mentor kids in a religious setting.

5. Make inspirational Youtube videos.

As we all know, kids love Youtube. One way to help and inspire the next generation is to create inspirational Youtube videos. You could make videos on a wide range of topics, from motivational speeches to tutorials on how to be a better student or a better person, for example.

6. Be a real example: Help them have good, moral fun, being a great inspiration and fun to be around.

Another important thing you can do to help the next generation of America is to be a real-life example. Help them have good, moral fun, be a great inspiration and fun to be around. Be someone they can look up to and emulate.

7. Go to where they are and try to influence or do whatever creative thing you can think of.

If you want to help the next generation of America in a creative way, then consider going to where they are and trying to influence or do whatever creative thing you can think of. You could try music, social networking that is inspirational and spiritual, write "serious" but youthful drama that can be helpful to people younger than you, and so much more.

8. Teach about tragic, wasted lives... crime, drugs.

Sometimes the best way to help the next generation of America is to teach them about the dangers of tragic, wasted lives like crime and drugs. Through your teaching efforts, you can help kids avoid the pitfalls that can derail their future.

9. Show how "untouchable beauty" is a wasted life.

A surefire way to help the next generation is to show how "untouchable beauty" is a wasted life. People gawk at unattainable model beauty, but you could teach kids how to appreciate inner beauty instead.

10. Teach creativity.

Finally, teaching creativity to the next generation can be an excellent way to inspire and equip them to take on the future. Through creativity, kids can develop mental agility, critical thinking skills, and much more.

In conclusion, there are many ways you can help the next generation of America. Whether you choose to volunteer, become a teacher, lead a small group class, or make inspirational videos, you can make a difference in the lives of kids. So, get out there and start inspiring the future leaders of America!

How to Choose Your Weapon in Monster Hunter Freedom 2: a Guide to Melee and Range Weapons

Monster Hunter Freedom 2 is an action-packed game that requires good skills and strategies to defeat monsters. One crucial aspect of the game is choosing the right weapon for your character. Whether you prefer melee or range weapons, there are multiple options to consider. In this guide, we'll explore the different types of weapons available in Monster Hunter Freedom 2 and their pros and cons, so you can make an informed decision when selecting your weapon of choice.

MELEE WEAPONS.

1. Greatsword: This is one of the most powerful weapons available in the game, but it's also the slowest. It's a cutting weapon that allows you to sever the tails of monsters for an extra carve. The Greatsword packs a lot of raw power, making it an ideal choice for more experienced players who prefer to hit hard and slow.

2. Longsword: The Longsword is similar to the Greatsword but lighter and faster, making it easier to wield. It's just as powerful, if not stronger, than the Greatsword, but it requires more precision and skill to use effectively.

3. Hammer: In the opinion of many gamers, the Hammer is the top weapon in terms of power, speed, and effectiveness. It delivers punishing blows to monsters and allows players to stun them, opening up opportunities for more damage.

4. Hunting Horn: This weapon offers limitless possibilities and is an excellent support weapon. Players can play music to help their allies or themselves, making it a valuable asset in group hunts. It's also a good choice for solo players who want an extra edge in combat.

5. Sword and Shield: This is the easiest weapon to learn and use effectively. It's the weakest melee weapon in the game, but it compensates for this by being very fast and nimble. It's an excellent choice for players who prefer a hit-and-run playstyle and want the ability to use items while guarding.

6. Dual Swords: These are the fastest melee weapons in the game, with a little more attack power than the Sword and Shield. However, they lack the ability to guard, which makes them more challenging to use in certain situations. The Demonization mode makes them extremely sharp and fast, allowing players to do the Demon Dance, a very fast combo.

7. Lance: This weapon has the most powerful guard, along with the Gunlance. It does both impact and cutting damage, making it ideal for players who prefer turtle tactics. Its precision makes it easier to hit bosses' weak points, but its lack of mobility can make it challenging to use in small spaces. The charge ability is a good way to knock down certain monsters but uses a lot of stamina.

8. Gunlance: This is a lance with an ability that lets you fire shells. The ammo is infinite, and it has a special attack called "Wyvern fire" that deals HUGE damage but has a slow reloading time of 2 minutes.

RANGE WEAPONS.

1. Light Bowgun: This weapon offers high mobility but lacks the ability to guard and the power of the Heavy Bowgun.

2. Heavy Bowgun: This is similar to the Light Bowgun, but it has better damage and range. It lacks mobility, but players can upgrade it by attaching a power barrel, which increases the range, power, and accuracy, or a shield, which compensates for the lack of mobility by allowing players to guard.

3. Bow: At the beginning of the game, bows are weak, but they offer good mobility, high elemental damage, and infinite arrows. The ability to dodge enemies' attacks quickly and do some damage makes them an excellent option for players who prefer a hit-and-run playstyle. Power coating increases the raw damage, while Paralysis Poison and Sleep coating allow players to paralyze, poison, or put monsters to sleep, respectively, for extra damage. Expert players can dodge all attacks, making them the best advantage of the bow.

Choosing the right weapon in Monster Hunter Freedom 2 is essential to your success. Players should consider their playstyle, experience level, and the types of monsters they will be facing before making their selection. Once you've chosen your weapon, it's time to head out and take down some monsters!

Dealing with the after effects of rape can be an overwhelming and challenging process. It's crucial to take steps to care for yourself and seek help from trusted individuals and professionals. This article will cover various tips and strategies to help you navigate the aftermath of this traumatic experience. 

Method 1: Immediate Response 

1. Call emergency services if you are in immediate danger. If you feel unsafe or threatened, call emergency services right away. 

2. Go to a safe place. Find a place where you feel safe and secure. 

3. Find a trusted friend or family member. Reach out to someone you trust, who can offer you emotional support and guidance. 

4. Visit or call a rape crisis center. These centers provide counseling, advocacy, and support to survivors of sexual assault. 

5. Preserve evidence. If you plan to report the assault to the police, preserve any evidence by avoiding bathing or washing any clothes. 

6. See your doctor. Your doctor may offer medical treatment for any physical injuries and assess your risk for sexually transmitted infections (STIs) and pregnancy. 

7. Take a trusted friend with you. Consider bringing a trusted friend or family member with you to your medical appointment. 

8. Get a thorough physical examination. A medical exam can assess any physical damage and collect evidence if necessary. 

9. Get tested for STIs. If you're at risk for STIs, it's essential to get tested to ensure timely treatment. 

10. Take the morning-after pill. If you're at risk of pregnancy, the morning-after pill can prevent pregnancy if taken within 72 hours of the assault. 

11. Take a pregnancy test. If you suspect you may be pregnant, take a pregnancy test to determine your options. 

Method 2: Dealing with the Emotional Aftermath 

1. Talk to a trusted friend. It's important to have someone to confide in and who can offer emotional support. 

2. Visit or call a rape crisis center. A rape crisis center can offer counseling, support groups, and other resources to help you cope. 

3. Terminate the pregnancy if you choose. If you decide to terminate the pregnancy, discuss your options with your doctor. 

4. Know you are the ultimate decision-maker. The decision to terminate the pregnancy or seek legal action is ultimately up to you. 

5. Take your time. Healing takes time and patience. It's okay to take things slow and prioritize your mental and physical well-being. 

6. Understand possible effects after a rape. PTSD, anxiety, depression, and other symptoms of trauma are common after a sexual assault. 

7. Understand the main symptoms of rape trauma syndrome. RTS is a psychological reaction to sexual assault, characterized by several symptoms such as denial, anger, anxiety, depression, and fear. 

8. Pay attention to your avoidance behaviors. Avoiding situations that trigger memories of the assault is common, but it can also limit your ability to cope. 

9. Find a counselor with experience in dealing with rape cases. Look for a mental health professional with experience in helping survivors of sexual assault. 

10. Talk with a therapist about problem-focused active coping. Active coping strategies involve taking specific actions to solve problems, such as seeking legal action or joining a support group. 

11. Avoid emotion-focused coping techniques. Avoiding emotions and using distraction techniques can delay healing and exacerbate symptoms. 

12. Ask your family and friends to seek counseling. Family and friends can also suffer from secondary traumatization, so encourage them to seek counseling themselves. 

13. Talk with your therapist about medications. Medication may help alleviate some symptoms of trauma, but it's not a substitute for therapy. 

14. Ask about prolonged exposure therapy. This is a therapy technique for PTSD that confronts the traumatic memories, thereby decreasing their impact. 

15. Ask about eye movement desensitization reprocessing. EMDR is a therapy technique meant to reprocess traumatic memories to lessen their impact. 

16. Try stress inoculation therapy. Situations that provoke anxiety and feelings of being unsafe may be “pleasantly prevented” by this form of therapy. 

Method 3: Self Care Techniques 

1. Talk with supportive friends and family. Lean on loved ones for emotional support and companionship. 

2. Stay away from people who invalidate your experience. It is best to stay away around people who deny or invalidate your experience. 

3. Join a support group. Sometimes it can be beneficial to join a support group to receive support from others who have undergone similar experiences. 

4. Focus on eating right. Eating a healthy diet can help fuel your body and mind. 

5. Stay physically active. Exercise can help reduce stress and boost your mood. 

6. Try mindfulness exercises. Mindfulness exercises such as deep breathing or meditation can be helpful in reducing stress. 

7. Practice yoga. Practicing yoga can help you connect your mind and body and help you manage stress and anxiety. 

8. Try yoga nidra meditation. Yoga nidra meditation is a guided mediation that helps you achieve deep relaxation and reduce anxiety. 

9. Spend time in nature. Being in nature can help reduce stress and boost your mood. 

10. Focus on your studies or work. Keeping busy can help maintain a sense of routine and structure. 

11. Read inspirational books. Reading inspirational literature can help you feel uplifted and hopeful. 

12. Recognize if you have an eating disorder. Eating disorders are often related to trauma. Seeking appropriate medical and psychological care for recovery is essential. 

13. Recognize if you’re self-harming. If you are self-harming, it's important to seek professional help and learn healthy coping mechanisms. 

14. See a sex therapist. A sex therapist can help you explore any intimacy issues, improve your relationships, and overcome any negative feelings about sexual experiences. 

Dealing with the after-effects of rape can be overwhelming, but you are not alone. Remember that healing is possible, and there are ample resources available to help survivors cope. Seeking help is the first step in the healing process, and self-care practices can help alleviate any emotional or physical symptoms.

How to Remotely Shutdown a Computer

If you need to shut down a computer remotely, there are several ways to do it depending on your operating system and network setup. Whether you're using a Windows, Linux, or Mac computer, you can follow these steps to remotely shut down a computer.

Method 1: Remotely Shutdown a Windows Computer

1. Open the Start menu on the computer you want to be able to shut down remotely. 

2. Type .services.msc while the Start menu is open and press ↵ Enter.

3. Find "Remote Registry" in the list of services. 

4. Right-click "Remote Registry" and select "Properties."

5. Select "Automatic" from the "Startup type" menu. 

6. Click the Start button again and type "firewall."

7. Click "Allow and app or feature through Windows Firewall." 

8. Click the "Change settings" button.

9. Check the "Windows Management Instrumentation" box.

10. Open the Command Prompt on your computer. 

11. Type .shutdown /i and press ↵ Enter.

12. Click the "Add" button. 

13. Enter in the computer name.

14. Set your shutdown options. 

15. Click "OK" to shut down the remote computers.

Method 2: Remotely Shut Down a Linux Computer

1. Prepare the remote computer for remote shutdown.

2. Find the remote computer's IP address. 

3. Open the Terminal on the Linux computer.

4. Install Samba. 

5. Run the remote shutdown command.

Method 3: Remotely Shut Down a Mac Computer

1. Open the Terminal on another Mac on your network.

2. Type .ssh username@ipaddress. 

3. Enter your user password for the remote Mac when prompted.

4. Type .sudo /sbin/shutdown now and press ⏎ Return. 

5. Click anywhere on your blank desktop.

6. Press .Alt+F4 while logged in remotely. 

7. Select "Shut down" from the drop down menu.

8. Click "OK" to shut down the computer.

Now you know how to remotely shut down a computer using different methods depending on the operating system. This can be useful for managing multiple computers or in situations where you need to turn off a computer remotely. Always use caution when shutting down a computer remotely and make sure you have the necessary permissions to do so.

Flying jets in Battlefield 2 can be a thrilling and rewarding experience, but it requires a certain level of skill and strategy to be truly effective. In this article, we'll take you through the steps to fly jets effectively in Battlefield 2, from binding keys to engaging enemy targets.

1. Start off by 'binding' keys. You will need a key to switch your view to behind, and a key to revert back to the cockpit view. This will allow you to quickly and easily switch between viewpoints, which is essential for engaging both air and ground targets.

2. Select your favorite kit. Any kills in jets will also count toward your kills for that kit, so it's important to choose a kit that complements your jet play style.

3. Select your jet. Different jets have different strengths and weaknesses, so it's important to choose the right one for your play style. Experiment with different jets until you find one that works for you.

4. Join or make a squad with other pilots in it, and use VOIP. This way, you can warn them when they are being engaged, or ask for support when the enemy is attacking you. It's important to work together with other pilots to be truly effective.

5. DO NOT fly the same routes. This will make it easier for the enemy to track you and shoot you down. Maneuver unpredictably and use cover to your advantage.

6. Now, this is where we tell you how to evade enemy pilots and engage them back. When your lock-on light starts to blink, immediately gain altitude and switch to the rear view. As soon as his missiles are Fox-Two, deploy your flares and take evasive action.

7. Engaging air targets: Keep an eye out for enemy planes and helicopters. When engaging them, try to get on their six and fire your missiles. Always use evasive maneuvers to avoid their fire.

8. Engaging ground targets: Fly high (around 150) and use your bombing mode to find targets. Give yourself a long run to your target, fire your machine gun as you approach, and drop bombs when necessary. Bombing accuracy comes with time, so practice until you get it right.

9. When the time comes for you to bail, try to aim your jet where you intend to land. The explosion will take out any enemy down there. Jump out and chute down.

10. Landing and taking off: Taking off is easy, but to do it fast, just use afterburners until you lift off. Landing requires more finesse. Reduce speed (different jets have different times), level off and land smoothly.

By following these steps, you'll be able to fly jets effectively in Battlefield 2 and dominate the skies. Remember to work together with your squad and always keep your eyes peeled for enemy targets. Good luck and happy flying!
